racecards.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Apprentice Handicap

runners.owner: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

prev_owners.owner: Teme Valley, J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

runners.comment: 0-12 and disappointing at Goodwood latest but he was knocking on the door before that
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and was disappointing at Goodwood last time but he had three seconds in a row this summer including on soft ground at Windsor (1m); has claims if he can recapture that form and he's not ruled out on this drop back in grade.

runners.comment: Ideally suited by good or slower and he won over C&D in May; dangerous back at this track
runners.spotlight: Usually comes from off the pace and he ended a long losing run when scoring off 2lb lower over C&D in May; mixed form in three runs since but he still looks feasibly treated and is ideally suited by good or slower ground; dangerous back at this track.

runners.comment: Mixed results since last win in March and was down the field latest; others preferred
runners.spotlight: Nine of his 12 wins have been on AW; ran well to finish fourth over C&D last month but he's been up and down since his last win in March and was beaten 6l at Chelmsford last time; others are more persuasive.

quotes.race: Bombardier Golden Beer Handicap
quotes.quote: It's a unique surface so previous form at the course helps a lot. Love Your Work is declared for Friday and as long as he's all right, he'll run again - Tim Fitzgerald, trainer.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Home Of The Odds Boost Handicap
quotes.quote: Adam said go forward. Love Your Work did have a look, he was a bit lairy in front, but the blinkers have done the job for him. I knew I had to put on a strong gallop. My horse will be better the next time and probably even better dropped back to seven furlongs - Robbie Walsh, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: S Barton & Exors of the Late W McLuskey

runners.comment: Admirable veteran has form figures of 5334144122 this season; respected
runners.spotlight: Career win number 17 when scoring over C&D last month and he's finished runner-up in both starts since; was no match for the winner at Carlisle last time but he still looks on a workable mark and this admirable veteran has form figures of 5334144122 this season; respected.

quotes.race: MansionBet Beaten By A Head Claiming Stakes
quotes.quote: Tony (Carroll) thought Poetic Force would need the run after such a long layoff so fair play to him. They didn't go any pace which helped. David Probert - Jockey

quotes.race: 32Red Casino Handicap
quotes.quote: We have not had Poetic Force that long and I am still learning about him in some ways. He has done that well and is the type we will keep going on the grass when the spring comes - Tony Carroll, trainer.

quotes.race: Betway Middle Distance Handicap
quotes.quote: I've ridden Poetic Force at home and he has a nice turn of foot. They didn't go mad and I thought it played into our hands - George Downing, jockey.

quotes.race: 32Red.com Claiming Stakes
quotes.quote: Poetic Force went up 9lb for winning which we thought was a bit harsh so we dropped him to a claimer. This is his trip and he'll get further - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

quotes.race: 32RedSport.com Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Poetic Force had a good draw and was very straightforward.  The race worked out nicely just having a tow off the leaders.  Adam's horse picked up but my lad always had it covered - Richard Kingscote, jockey.

runners.comment: Went close over C&D last month and same story on AW on Saturday; key player
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been gained at shorter trips but she's gone close over C&D and at Lingfield (1m, AW) in last two starts; this is a quick turnaround after her run on Saturday but she's on same mark and is a big player if she can repeat that form.

quotes.race: Billy Wilber 80th Stakes Handicap
quotes.quote: I was a bit worried about the ground this morning because I was here yesterday when we had all that rain. Bear To Dream hadn't run for a while so I was worried about her getting caught out on that tacky ground, but she was obviously fitter than I thought she was. She's very straightforward. She does plenty at home and Kaiya's [Fraser] has given her a great ride. That's his first winner for us and we've been using him for a bit. He has a real nice way of riding - Michael Attwater, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing

runners.comment: Two placed efforts this summer but he didn't fire last time and his last win was in 2023
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and heavy; returned from a break with placed efforts at Windsor (1m) and Brighton (7f) this summer but his form cooled at Lingfield 16 days ago; bit to prove again after that and his last win was two years ago.

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo and he's a possible improver on handicap debut; needs watching in market
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l or more at increasingly big prices in three qualifying runs (6f-1m) but he now goes handicapping off a lowly mark and is a possible improver back up in trip; needs watching in market.

runners.comment: Went close over C&D (good to firm) latest and has leading claims if he can back that up
runners.spotlight: Sole win was 15 months ago but that was off 7lb higher and he returned to form with a near-miss over C&D last time; has leading claims if he can back that up but best form has been on good/good to firm and it would be a worry if the ground is testing.

runners.comment: Well held at big prices in all four runs including a 1m handicap; best watched
runners.spotlight: Well held at big prices in all four runs including on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month; still unexposed but can only be watched until she shows more positive signs.

prev_owners.owner: Paul Kwok, Angel Li & Orbital Racing Group

runners.comment: Suited by good or slower and was a rallying fourth over 7f (AW) last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Has modest strike-rate of 3-50 but he rallied for a close fourth when favourite in classified event at Lingfield (7f, AW) last time; looks interesting on this step back up in trip and he's very effective on slow ground.

runners.comment: Placed three times here this season and had an excuse at 1m2f last time; could go well
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but he's been placed three times here (7f/1m) this season and had an excuse over 1m2f last time; could go well back in trip and he's versatile trip-wise; yard also runs Poetic Force.

quotes.race: alex@alexdonohuepr.co.uk Classified Claiming Stakes
quotes.quote: Voltaic has relished the ground and relaxed over that trip - Sean Levey, rider.

runners.comment: 0-12 on turf but she's been running well in this sphere and shouldn't be far away
runners.spotlight: Much better known for AW exploits but she's reached the frame in last four turf runs and last two have been over C&D; record in this sphere is now 0-12 but she seems fairly versatile ground-wise and shouldn't be far away.

quotes.race: Play 4 To Win At Betway Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Laurentia probably had the run of the race. She's done really well this year. I'd hope she could win again. She's been very progressive - George Downing, jockey.

runners.comment: Won this race last year and he also scored at Bath on Saturday; respected under penalty
runners.spotlight: Has modest strike-rate of 3-54 but he won this race (soft) last year and was also successful at Bath (1m, good to soft) on Saturday; that was a narrow win but he kept on well to get on top close home and is respected under penalty.

runners.comment: In good form until a heavy defeat at Windsor latest; needs to bounce back
runners.spotlight: Came good with narrow win in first-time cheekpieces over C&D (good to firm) in June; held his form in next four starts but his good run came to a halt at Windsor last time; had some traffic issues in that race but he needs to bounce back near best; hood is removed and cheekpieces return.

runners.comment: Just 1-14 and still has something to prove on slow ground; not the percentage call
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth in classified event here (7f, good to firm) on stable debut in June; this step back up in trip looks a positive but she's now 1-14 and still has something to prove on slow ground; mixed messages.

racecards.verdict: Preference is for JOHNJAY who is ideally suited by good or slower ground and is only 2lb higher than for his C&D win in May.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/2 Strobelight, 13/2 Never Dream,  James Park Woods, 7/1 Bear To Dream, 8/1 Poetic Force, 9/1 Joycean Way, 10/1 Johnjay,  Gone Rogue,  Kessaar Power, 14/1 Voltaic, 16/1 Laurentia,  Clipsham Noble,  Perfect Parole, 20/1 Love Your Work, 25/1 Grey Selkie

racecards.race_name: blackmorebuild.com EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: 0-5 but sets fair standard back in a maiden and yard is 25% with 2yos here in recent years
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden but was close third in Newbury novice (6f, good to firm) in July and she ran respectably when third of five in a Sandown nursery (7f, soft) 16 days ago; sets a fair standard back in a maiden and yard is 25% with 2yos here in recent years; key player.

runners.comment: Well-bred gelding but he made a low-key start at Kempton and needs major improvement
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns foal; half-brother to plenty of useful winners but he made a low-key start at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; ran green and lost a shoe in that race but he needs major improvement on this switch to turf; hood added.

runners.comment: 25,000euros breeze-up 2yo; has good pedigree and needs watching in market on debut
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros breeze-up 2yo; third foal; half-brother to Swedish 7f winner American Lily (including 2yo); dam US 6f AW 2yo winner, sister to 6.5f/7f winner First Down, half-sister to winners Gold City (smart 6f/7f including 2yo) and Clear Water (7f Listed); has good pedigree and he needs watching in market on debut.

runners.comment: Half-brother to a useful winner and represents in-form yard; needs a close look on debut
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners Raja Raja (1m4f AW; RPR 104) and Chola Empire (7f AW including 2yo; 85); dam 6f/7f AW winner (including 2yo; 83), out of unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Keraka; represents in-form yard and he needs a close look on debut.

runners.owner: A Bevan, I McMillan & Glassex Holdings
runners.comment: Didn't show much at 50-1 on recent Salisbury debut (6f, good to soft); can only be watched
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much at 50-1 on recent Salisbury debut (6f, good to soft) and can only be watched after that tailed-off effort.

runners.comment: 25,000gns yearling; plenty to like on paper and he's an interesting newcomer
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Snoano (useful 1m-1m4f including 2yo) and Tulip Dress (6f/7f), out of unraced half-sister to French 1m1f Group 3 winner Sparkling Beam; plenty to like on paper and he's an interesting newcomer.

runners.comment: Well-held fourth on Yarmouth debut and needs a transformation for new yard
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and finished some way off the three market leaders on her Yarmouth debut (7f, good) in July; should know more this time but she needs a transformation after a short break; left Mick Appleby and is now with Alice Haynes.

racecards.verdict: The vote goes to AIRSIDE, who sets a fair standard and represents a yard with a 25% record with 2yos here in recent years.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Airside, 3/1 White Deer, 5/1 Rajendra,  Hala Samar, 10/1 Attention Seeker,  Quarter, 25/1 Tinsel

racecards.race_name: Blackmore Design & Build Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Regressive form in her four runs and was last of eight on nursery debut at Chelmsford
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when sixth on Goodwood debut (6f, good) but she's taken three backward steps since and was last of eight on recent nursery debut at Chelmsford (6f, AW); has something to prove and she needs a transformation back on turf.

runners.comment: Two respectable runs in nurseries and he's not ruled out back in trip; cheekpieces added
runners.spotlight: No progress in five starts but he's run respectably in two nurseries, more recently when fifth of 12 at Newbury (7f, good) 18 days ago; cheekpieces are now added and he could get involved if he can find some extra spark back in trip.

runners.owner: Ian Wight, Tony Lyons And Ptnr
runners.comment: Won on slow ground at Catterick in July and was runner-up here last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Won a Catterick nursery (6f, good to soft) in July; not seen to quite the same effect in three subsequent runs but she chased home an improver here (5.3f) last time and this return to slower ground looks a positive; in the mix back up in trip.

quotes.race: Happy 80th Birthday Lesley Mullis Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: The first part of the race went very smoothly and I was going to come around them turning in but Shetakesthegold hung quite badly the whole of the last two furlongs. Hopefully she's a bit babyish and it's her first time being in front as well. If she gets out of that I think she'll improve again - Alex Voikhansky, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has been well held in last two starts and others are preferred
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has been well held in his last two nurseries and was behind Shetakesthegold here two weeks ago; mark continues to fall but others are preferred; first-time cheekpieces replace visor.

runners.comment: Landed a gamble on nursery debut over C&D 15 days ago; big player again up 4lb
runners.spotlight: Struggled in her qualifying runs but she looked a different proposition on nursery debut when landing a gamble over C&D (good; 11-8 favourite) last month; up 4lb for that success but she showed a good attitude to beat Could Be The Move and is open to more progress; all of her runs have been on good/good to firm but she's a half-sister to some soft ground winners.

runners.comment: 0-6 but was close second behind Arvana Belle over C&D last time; respected
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but she ran her best race so far when a front-running second behind Arvana Belle over C&D 15 days ago; has a 3lb swing in the weights with her old rival and she could make another bold bid; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.

racecards.verdict: The most striking contender is ARVANA BELLE, who showed determination when landing a gamble over C&D on her nursery debut last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Arvana Belle, 7/2 Could Be The Move, 4/1 Shetakesthegold, 6/1 Madman, 13/2 Too Hot To Love, 9/1 Beach Partee

racecards.race_name: Blackmore Equestrian Construction Handicap

runners.comment: Off the mark with front-running win at Leicester; only 1lb higher here and he's respected
runners.spotlight: Finished close up in his first three runs this season before making the breakthrough (11th attempt) at Leicester (1m4f, good) in July; that was a narrow front-running win but he's only 1lb higher here and is still unexposed at this trip; respected.

runners.comment: Close up in this race last year and he won by 3l over 1m2f here last time; big player
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been gained at Brighton and latest was by 3l over 1m2f (good to firm) last month; 4lb rise for that success looks fair and he finished a close third on soft ground in this race last year; big player again back up in trip.

quotes.race: Vickers.Bet For Best Prices Guaranteed Handicap
quotes.quote: Fighting Poet doesn't wear blinkers for fashion purposes and things had to go right, but he's got the talent to run well. After his last run, he seems to be improving a little bit recently and we've changed his training regime a little bit. Leah [Brundle], who looks after him, has done a great job. They're a great couple and I'm delighted for them. I'm also delighted for the Adlestrop Club. They are wonderfully enthusiastic people and we've had some fun over the years and it's nice to finally get his head in front for them - Trainer Richard Phillips, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Emphatic win at Lingfield in June but his form has nosedived in last two starts
runners.spotlight: Got back on the scoresheet with an emphatic win in classified event at Lingfield (11.6f) in June; ran to similar level when runner-up over C&D last month but his form has nosedived in last two runs; now drops back in grade but has a bit to prove again and he's a frequent slow starter.

runners.comment: Won over 1m2f here last month but no impact in follow-up bid; others preferred
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with clearcut success here (1m2f classified) last month but she couldn't make an impact on her return visit (1m) two weeks ago; still looks feasibly treated on her penultimate form but she's not easy to predict and has never won over this far; mixed messages.

quotes.race: Human League Playing Live After Racing Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Celebrating Ethel learned a lot the last day and got a lovely ride from Luke. We might even go back to a mile with her. She is a nice filly and I think she can improve again - Andrew Kinirons, trainer.

runners.comment: Won over C&D last month but that was on good to firm and slow ground would be a concern
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door before winning over C&D last month and he produced a strong finish to beat Irezumi in that race; remains feasibly treated off 4lb higher but most of his turf runs have been on good to firm and it would be a concern if conditions are on the slow side.

quotes.race: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com Classified Stakes
quotes.quote: Looking back at his form Foinix is well capable of having a sudden change in fortunes. He feels like he's really enjoying his racing. At Brighton he didn't look like a classified horse and didn't do so again today. He can just be a little bit gassy but he gets into a beautiful rhythm. He's definitely worth a go at further trips, but keep him where he is for now because he's doing nothing wrong - Joe Leavy, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

prev_owners.owner: J Palmer-Brown, M Daniels And Partner

runners.comment: Placed here last twice but he still has stamina to prove at this trip; reservations
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has been placed here (1m2f/1m) in his last two starts but he's not won on turf since 2022 and he still has stamina to prove at this trip; blinkers are removed and visor returns.

runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he's not finished closer than 10l to a winner; hood now added
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo but he's not finished closer than 10l to a winner, including in a Windsor handicap (11.4f, good to firm) last month; that was after a break but he needs a transformation with hood added.

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo and he's a possible improver on handicap debut; needs close look in market
runners.spotlight: Struggled in his three qualifying runs (1m2f/1m) but he now goes handicapping with headgear added and this unexposed 3yo is a possible improver at this new trip; needs close look in market.

runners.comment: C&D win on penultimate run and could resume his progress back on turf; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle (2m1f, good) in June and a classified event over C&D last month; well held in a Lingfield handicap (1m4f, AW) last time but he still has potential after only eight starts and could resume his progress back on turf.

runners.comment: 0-7 but fair fourth at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) latest and has claims if he can build on that
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-7 but he ran his best race so far when fourth of 12 in classified event at Lingfield (1m4f, AW; tongue-tie added) last month; that was his first try at this trip and he has claims if he can build on that.

racecards.verdict: The vote goes to FIGHTING POET, who was an emphatic winner here last month and a 4lb rise for that success looks fair.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Fighting Poet, 9/2 Ricardo Phillips, 5/1 Foinix, 6/1 Zaeim Alharb, 15/2 Both Of Us, 8/1 Silk And Steel, 10/1 Celebrating Ethel, 14/1 Irezumi,  Headshot, 25/1 Fornido

racecards.race_name: Blackmore Compliance Solutions Handicap

runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; looks feasibly treated on best form and is not ruled out back in trip
runners.spotlight: Best form in 7f Salisbury novice last month where he raced freely but kept on well in third of nine, despite being hampered; well-held fifth on handicap debut at Newbury since but that was behind a major improver over 1m; still unexposed after only six starts and he could go well back in trip.

runners.comment: Has won here and on AW in last two starts and he's open to more progress; key player
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he's won four times this season including here (6f, good to firm) and at Lingfield (7f, AW) in last two starts; has penalty for that last success but he's open to more progress and has a record of 21221 at this track; most turf runs have been on good to firm but he's run respectably on soft.

runners.comment: Won at Newbury in May but he's lost his way since and needs a major revival
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner who handles any ground but he can miss the break; now 2lb lower than for his Newbury win (1m, good) in May but he's lost his way since and needs a major revival.

runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he's hard to predict and was beaten 10l on AW latest; risks attached
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-11 and has been hard to predict; well handicapped on his best form but he was beaten 10l at Southwell (7f, AW; 9-2) last month and needs to bounce back after that disappointing effort; blinkers removed.

runners.comment: Versatile mare who has been running well since July and she's respected back in trip
runners.spotlight: Won at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) in July and she's held her form since, including when runner-up over 1m at Salisbury (good to soft) last time; should go well again back in trip and she won't mind what the weather does; blinkers back on.

quotes.race: Peter Britton 60 Years Racing At Salisbury Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: Silent Flame is a nice, lovely filly with a good attitude - she'd probably be Al Kazeem's fastest sprinters. She's improving all the time and has thrived this year. She's owned by Gloria Abbey who is very enthusiastic, she's been in the game a long time and is well into her eighties. She knows more about the game than I do - Rod Millman, trainer.

runners.comment: In good form on turf this summer but was well held by Kranjcar on AW last Monday
runners.spotlight: Clearcut win over 7f at Lingfield (soft) in June; finished close up on fast and slow ground in next two starts but he didn't really fire on AW last week and was almost 4l behind Kranjcar; bit to prove again after that and others are more persuasive; change of headgear.

stable_tour.quote: For a horse who was sourced for only 1,000gns at the Newmarket Sales [Book 4] he's done us proud. Ardad has made a fantastic start to his career and I don't think we will be able to afford any of his progeny this year at the sales. It is a very busy time for us in fact I remember last year we looked 430 horses for the sales and bought around 20, so you don't get these bargains without your research and we have a team of us that spend a lot of hours doing just that. We took him to Bath first time out where he won, not totally surprising as his work had been good. I remember asking the handicapper in the spring what rating he thought I would need to get in the 7f nursery at Glorious Goodwood so we planned on that as his big target. But Lady Luck deserted him in the race as after a furlong he clipped heals and almost fell over, only to regain his composure and finish with a right old flourish to be a fast finishing second. He would have got up in another ten strides. Jason Weaver mentioned it on TV how unlucky we were and what a big thing it would be for a small yard to win at such a prestigious meeting. He was fifth over 7f at Salisbury last week but he should get a mile in time as he's so laidback. 15-09-21

racecards.verdict: This can go to KRANJCAR, who has resumed his progress with two recent wins and has form figures of 21221 at Brighton.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Kranjcar, 7/2 Silent Flame, 6/1 Rockstar Icon, 13/2 Tenbob Tommy, 7/1 Blue Collar Lad, 10/1 Marsh Benham

racecards.race_name: Streamline Always Leading The Way 'Confined' Handicap (Not Won During The 2025 Flat Turf Season)

runners.comment: Acts on any ground ans showed sign of revival when clear second at Epsom; big player
runners.spotlight: Well below best form this season and remains without a win since May 2023 but took big step back in right direction when clear second at Epsom (6f, good) three weeks ago; has leading claims if he can back that up and he's versatile ground-wise

quotes.race: tote £100K Guaranteed Placepot Every Day Handicap
quotes.quote: Danger Alert has been an enigma. William knows him very well now and he needs to get there late - the interference probably helped him because otherwise he might have got there too soon - and he puts his head up a little - George Boughey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: You cannot fault him this year as he has already won three times, including last time at Chester over 5f. He never does a huge amount at home so is difficult to gauge, but the important thing is he saves his best for the track. He has shown a liking for slower ground, but I don't think quicker going will be a problem as he's a very versatile. I never saw him getting as high as 94, but he is in good shape and his target next week is the Palace of Holyroodhouse Handicap. 14-06-23

runners.comment: 0-9 in handicaps but she's on a reduced mark and could be dangerous back on turf
runners.spotlight: Won a York novice (5f, good to soft) last June but she's 0-9 in handicaps since; mixed record in five runs this season but she's on a reduced mark and was runner-up behind an emphatic winner at Lingfield on penultimate run; could be dangerous back on turf.

runners.comment: On reduced mark but he's been out of sorts for current yard and needs to get back on track
runners.spotlight: First two wins were on turf (6f) and latest was on AW (7f) in March; now 6lb lower than for that last success but he's been out of sorts in four runs for current yard and needs to get back on track; tongue-tie removed.

runners.comment: Has struggled in three runs for current yard and needs a big turnaround
runners.spotlight: Three wins (5.6f/7f) for the Kublers and latest was at this track last August; well handicapped on that form but she's struggled in three runs for current yard and needs a big turnaround.

runners.comment: Disappointing at Yarmouth (7f) last time but has possibilities if he can bounce back
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who is 0-14 on turf; finished last of nine at Yarmouth (7f) last time but he was running well before that including when third at Yarmouth (5f, good) on his penultimate run; has possibilities if he can recapture that form and he's not ruled out back in trip.

runners.comment: 3-45 and none too reliable these days; comes with risks attached from out of the weights
runners.spotlight: 3-45 and none too reliable these days; finished well when runner-up over C&D (good) last month but he laboured at Lingfield (sixth of nine) last Monday; has something to prove again after that and he's 3lb out of the weights.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is DANGER ALERT (nap) who turned things around with a clear second at Epsom and is a big player if he can back that up.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Danger Alert, 7/2 Havana Gila, 5/1 Court Of Session, 6/1 Fai Fai, 8/1 The Organiser, 14/1 Monkey Miss

racecards.race_name: Brighton Racecourse Christmas Parties Handicap

runners.comment: Losing run is up to 13 and she was out the back at Haydock ten days ago; tongue-tie added
runners.spotlight: Very well treated on best form last season but losing run is up to 13 and she beaten only one rival in big field at Haydock ten days ago; that was a quick turnaround after a respectable effort over C&D but she's not easy to predict and others are more convincing; tongue-tie added.

quotes.race: Trade Centre UK Handicap
quotes.quote: I can't believe the amount Jacquelina has improved. It's kind of by default that she has because she ran on the heavy ground at Windsor and we didn't think she'd like it, but since she's run on soft ground she's just improved. To go and win like that today under a double penalty was pretty impressive. I wake up at about 8st 6lb and this saddle is about 2st! She's got an entry at Hamilton on Thursday but there's also a couple of options at Thirsk for her - Grace McEntee, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Won this race in 2023 and 2024; ran well at Bath yesterday; player if taking his chance
runners.spotlight: Won this race in 2023 and 2024, and this 7yo has been running well recently including when runner-up at Bath yesterday; handles any ground and he's a big player if taking his chance here.

quotes.race: Get Your Ladbrokes Daily Odds Boost Handicap
quotes.quote: Secret Handsheikh was a convincing winner last time and went up 9lb but he loves this surface. He skips off the surface and has such a good attitude - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore
runners.comment: Made most for a comfortable win at Chepstow (5f, soft); close second at Bath yesterday
runners.spotlight: Doubled his win tally when making most to score off a reduced mark at Chepstow (5f, soft) 13 days ago; that was a comfortable success and he remains feasibly treated; showed he's still in good form with close second at Bath yesterday.

stable_tour.quote: He was second at Bath the other day. He could end up being a little bit ground dependent. You wouldn't probably run him on anything with firm in it but he'll be a horse who'll make a name for himself as the year goes on. There's plenty more to come from him on ground with a bit of juice in it over six or seven furlongs. 04-07-24

runners.comment: Last win was 17 months ago but was a creditable third over 6f here last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and good to soft; last win was 17 months ago but she ran well under prominent ride to finsh third here (6f) two weeks ago; on unchanged mark and she's in the mix on this drop in trip; cheekpieces reapplied.

quotes.race: QuinnBet Acca Bonus Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: Unico is probably still a little bit green and a bit on her nerves. When she got to the front she was still a bit unsure about what to do but I think she did it nicely. We've had a little downpour which has probably helped - Josie Gordon, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Improver who has won his last two starts including a dominant display over C&D; key player
runners.spotlight: Most of his turf runs have been on good/good to firm but he's run respectably on soft; made his breakthrough (16th attempt) with cheekpieces added at Lingfield (5f, good) last month and he followed up with a dominant display over C&D 15 days ago; up another 8lb but he's on a roll and was as good as ever last time; key player.

runners.comment: On much reduced mark but he's struggled this year and needs a major revival
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his Windsor win (5f, good to soft) last April but he's struggled this year and was beaten 11l here (6f) last time; lots to prove and he needs a major revival.

quotes.race: Star Property Group Handicap
quotes.quote: Antiphon is a legend of a horse, just so easy to train and he's run seven times for us since we bought him in February and he hasn't been out of the winner's circle. From where we were stood on the stands it looked like we'd been touched off, but he's so tough - Michael Keady, joint-trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He is a lovely colt who made a pleasing debut when third at Newmarket's Craven meeting over 5f. Unfortunately, he had a niggly issue subsequently and will not be out for a while. He looks a proper hardy two-year-old. He will be back later this summer. 19-05-21

runners.comment: All three wins on Polytrack but was disappointing at Chelmsford latest; down the list
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on AW and latest was at Lingfield (5f) in June; that was a brave success but she's been up and down since and was disappointing in classified event at Chelmsford last time; still lightly raced on turf but this is competitive back in a handicap and others are more solid.

runners.owner: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher

runners.spotlight: Placed in C&D handicaps in last two starts but was beaten 4l last time and her record now stands at 0-13; others look stronger.

stable_tour.quote: He won a bumper at Market Rasen in very good style in July and then nearly fell at the crossing turning in at Ludlow this month - Tom Cannon thought he'd have given the winner a run for his money otherwise. He had to have a joint stitched afterwards and he'll now be sent hurdling. 07-11-24

runners.comment: Hurdle winner who has the potential to improve on his sole Flat effort (debut)
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo who has raced mostly over hurdles, winning once for current stable; has the potential to improve on his sole Flat effort (modest fourth over 1m4f on debut).

quotes.race: Raceday Ready Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (Go North Grey Bomber Juvenile Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB)
quotes.quote: It was a messy, unsatisfactory race and Turn And Finish was well short of maximum fitness. He came over from Joseph [O'Brien, trainer] and we gave him a break when he got here. We just wanted to give him a race to help with our preparations for the Fred Winter. That's nowhere near his best and he'll be much better in a strongly run race coming off the pace. His Irish form also suggests that. It's job done, though, and all roads lead to the Cheltenham Festival. He'll be a much, much better horse in the middle of March. He jumps well and travels strongly and looks a hurdling type, and for most of that race he was looking around and thinking what was going on. I think 24 runners going flat out in front of him will be much better - Harry Derham, trainer.

runners.comment: Fared better at Ffos Las last time but needs another step forward
runners.spotlight: 40-1 shot in both outings, better effort when fourth upped to 1m4f at Ffos Las latest; needs another step forward and may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.

stable_tour.quote: He's been great and is from a family that has served us well with Sweet Reward and Two Tempting. He's flopped the last couple of times, which is a bit of a concern, but he seems healthy. He's risen up the weights a fair bit but I'd like to think there's more winning in him over a mile to 1m2f. Hopefully he'll run again at the end of the month. 20-08-25

He'll go up in the handicap like he did last time he won and that's where he seemed to struggle, but we'll probably go for a race at Musselburgh next. We got him at Newmarket when Ryan Tongue, who headhunts for Deva Racing and buys a lot of my horses, liked his profile and thought he could win races for us. He looked well handicapped, which is why we went for him - James Owen, trainer.

runners.comment: Made all at Newmarket last time; may improve further kept to 1m; respected

runners.comment: Has shown bits of promise but needs improvement to get off the mark

runners.spotlight: Record is just 1-8, the win over 7f on AW; has shaped as if 1m is worth exploring but failed to get home when upped to this trip last time; something to prove.

stable_tour.quote: She's been second twice now, including on her first run this season when beaten only a head at Wolverhampton by Crepe Suzette. Hopefully she'll be winning soon. She came eighth behind Liberate at Goodwood on Saturday where the ground and track possibly weren't ideal for her. 29-05-25

runners.comment: Ran creditably at Southwell last time, taking record in AW handicaps to 1132; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably from the front at Southwell (1m) returned to this sphere last time, taking record in AW handicaps to 1132; firmly in the mix.

runners.comment: Has shown his recent improvement on turf; return to AW presents a question mark
runners.spotlight: Below par turned out quickly last time and given a rest since; has shown his recent improvement on turf and this return to AW presents a question mark.

runners.comment: Record of 232 since switched to handicaps; he's showing clear signs that his turn is near

runners.spotlight: Didn't see out 1m on soft ground last time and that's best ignored; won her novice over C&D last autumn and this mark should be within range back on the AW.

racecards.verdict: A chance is taken on BRITISH BLUE, who's had excuses with one thing and another on turf this year. Bold Suitor is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Champion Again Ole, 9/2 Bold Suitor, 6/1 British Blue, 7/1 Silca Bay,  Ernie's Valentine, 8/1 Kit Gabriel, 10/1 Havana Sky,  Moby Quick, 20/1 Token Gesture

racecards.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Hurdles win came fresh and Rossa Ryan knows him well; one to consider back from a break
runners.spotlight: Last two wins have come in the autumn, including back from a layoff on his hurdling debut last November, so the four-month break isn't an offputter; Rossa Ryan knows him well but he needs a career-best effort off this mark on just his second AW start.

quotes.race: Watch Unbridled On attheraces.com Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We bought Campaign Trail at the sales last year and he had a hairline fracture on his pelvis literally the first day we schooled him, so we gave him the whole year off. Willie Mullins said to me at the time if he was his, then he'd be out in the field, although he did actually end up that way because we couldn't train him. They've [Its Only Money ownership group] been incredibly patient. It's a big investment and they've got something to look forward to - Kim Bailey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's an exciting young horse who had a good record on the Flat. He's ready to run over hurdles after a setback last year. He's done everything we've asked of him and I'm really looking forward to seeing him out. 19-11-24

prev_owners.owner: M Sample E Sample G Williams
Richard D A Hames & Mark Russell
W J and T C O Gredley

runners.comment: Not as good now; handicapped accordingly, though, and will be happier back on the AW
runners.spotlight: Winless since March 2023 and isn't as good now; handicapped accordingly, though, and will be happier back on the AW having shown more from a wide stall at Chester last time; still capable.

quotes.race: BetUK Over 40,000 Live Streamed Races Handicap
quotes.quote: I think the break has done Barenboim the world of good. He had a busy time of things and, while his last run was solid, he probably struggled to see out the two miles  - Jason Watson, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.breeder: C&s B/S Ltd, C Younghusband, K McGivern
runners.comment: Currently 0-9 on Polytrack but he's reliable and should go well again under Oisin Murphy
runners.spotlight: Landed a sixth win on Tapeta when making it 2-4 over this trip off 4lb lower last time; little room for manoeuvre off his revised mark and is currently 0-9 on Polytrack but he's reliable and should go well again under Oisin Murphy.

prev_owners.owner: M White, Mrs A Gillies And Partner

runners.comment: Back to mark just 1lb higher than when winning over C&D in July, and can better latest run
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard in first-time cheekpieces (off tonight) and paid for it late when well behind Sax Appeal on Tapeta last time; back to a mark just 1lb higher than when winning over C&D in July and should fare better.

runners.comment: Dry since a C&D win off 12lb higher early last year but can never be ruled out round here
runners.spotlight: Dry since a C&D win off 12lb higher early last year and will need to put a couple of below-par efforts behind him; the return to Polytrack will help, though, and he can never be ruled out round here.

racecards.verdict: Second in this race last autumn off an 11lb higher mark, RED FLYER is taken to bounce back now returned to the AW.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Sax Appeal, 3/1 Campaign Trail, 5/1 Kingdom Of Stars,  Red Flyer, 8/1 Barenboim

racecards.race_name: Free Bet With Unibet's Betbuilder Rebound Handicap

runners.comment: Better on her AW (9.5f, Tapeta) debut; holds claims on that if settling from a wide stall
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ian Williams maiden who was slowly away (has missed the break before) in a first-time visor when well beaten on her stable debut in July; much better on her AW (9.5f, Tapeta) debut last time and holds claims on that if settling from a wide stall.

runners.comment: Six-time winner over 1m round here; only beaten narrowly over C&D in June; each-way shout
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner over 1m round here in his younger days but nothing like as good now and has poor strike-rate in recent times (behind Carderock latest); only beaten narrowly over C&D in June, though, and holds each-way claims back into a 0-60.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (A Jockey Club Grassroots Mile Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Uzincso ran well here to be third last time so was entitled to run well. He was given a great ride by Neil Callan and we may come back here for something at a Friday night meeting - John Butler, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's better on the all-weather, where his mark of 83 is 5lb higher than on turf. Six of his seven wins have been on the all-weather, so it was particularly pleasing to see him win at Newmarket this summer over a mile on a day that meant a lot to his connections, being the Glenn Roeder raceday. I think the handicapper has been a little harsh on him as he never runs a bad race, always giving his best, which makes it tough to drop him. He's almost ready to go again on the all-weather and is one to side with over the winter months. 23-09-22

runners.breeder: Qatar Bloodstock Ltd, S A S U Ecurie De
runners.comment: Would want to see market confidence before considering him; returns to AW after a break
runners.spotlight: Missed more than a year before struggling in two runs in Dubai and four starts for this yard; sliding down the weights but would want to see market confidence before considering him; returns to the AW after a break.

runners.comment: Very inconsistent this year; switch from cheekpieces to blinkers has to make a difference
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden for two yards who's done a lot of his racing over further and proved wildly inconsistent this year; the switch from cheekpieces to blinkers will need to make a difference.

runners.comment: Consistent maiden; again gave his running over C&D latest; player in first-time headgear
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who again gave his running over C&D last time (Uzincso just behind) without necessarily improving for the step up from 1m2f; one to consider in first-time headgear taking a drop in class.

runners.comment: Maiden who's started big prices for all five runs and finished well beaten every time
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's started big prices for all five runs and finished well beaten every time, latterly off 3lb higher here (1m4f) having been gelded; goes in first-time blinkers tonight (wore a visor at Lingfield in June).

runners.comment: Dropped another 5lb but not easy to find any positives back from a short break
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in four runs this year, latterly when not looking to see out 1m4f at Newmarket; dropped another 5lb but not easy to find any positives back from a short break.

runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxxi And Partner
runners.comment: Better since returned to Polytrack; claims are obvious for a yard enjoying a fine spell
runners.spotlight: Better since returned to Polytrack, winning easily at Lingfield 12 days ago and running to a similar level under a penalty last Thursday; 1lb lower tonight, off her revised mark, and her claims are obvious for a yard enjoying a fine start to the month.

runners.comment: Regressive maiden (1m-1m6f); plenty of chances now and is some way down the pecking order
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden (1m-1m6f) who goes without cheekpieces back on the AW after a month off; had plenty of chances now and is some way down the pecking order.

runners.comment: Runs well fresh so the break looks in his favour; capable of popping up off this mark
runners.spotlight: Often runs well fresh, so the break looks in his favour, and two of his wins have come at big prices; capable of popping up off a career-low mark but he'll need to bounce back after a poor summer.

stable_tour.quote: He is unlucky to still be a maiden after five runs with form figures of 23224 illustrating how genuine he is. He ran well last time at Nottingham over 1m½f when only beaten a length, and with the winner rated 82 he is a high 70s performer already. I think he is better with a little cut in the ground - in fact the softer the better. We will stick to a mile, and I expect him to opening his account sooner rather than later. 25-05-22

runners.owner: M Webber, M T Ward & Schiff Family

prev_owners.owner: M Webber, M T Ward & Schiff Family

runners.comment: Should appreciate tonight's longest trip to date on latest evidence; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a useful French stayer who should appreciate tonight's longest trip to date on latest evidence; one to consider, having been dropped another 2lb, switched from a visor to first-time blinkers.

runners.comment: Bit more last time, having come in for support, but that was only a 0-50 classified event
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's endured a couple of lengthy absences and is still to finish placed after nine starts; offered a bit more last time, having come in for support, but that was only a 0-50 classified event.

runners.comment: Didn't look to see out 1m5f in first-time cheekpieces (retained) latest; others stronger
runners.spotlight: Didn't look to see out 1m5f in first-time cheekpieces (retained) last time having offered a bit more on his first start since being gelded previously; others hold more pressing claims.

racecards.verdict: The penny has finally dropped with CARDINAL POINT (nap) and she's  preferred to Northern Blaze and consistent maiden Carderock.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Cardinal Point, 5/1 Carderock, 6/1 Little Miss India, 8/1 Uzincso,  Northern Blaze, 10/1 Peter Piper, 12/1 Age Of Time,  Pure Theory, 16/1 Holy Smoker,  Hamdani Cruiser, 20/1 Night Horn, 25/1 Take The A Train, 33/1 Dubai Immo

racecards.race_name: Happy Birthday Dave Bramley Apprentice Handicap

runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson & Ollie Pears
runners.comment: Fair hurdler; yet to win in eight runs on the Flat; ideally suited by good/good to firm
runners.spotlight: Has won six of her 20 races over hurdles, the latest at Newton Abbot in July (2m1f, good); below-par over same C&D last time; yet to win in eight runs on the Flat, last time finishing sixth of 11 in a Catterick handicap (1m4f, good to firm) when coming back after a wind op; best on good/fast ground.

quotes.race: Smokey Joe Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Max Of Stars is a good mare and that's the best she's run. She's always been a keen-going sort, but she's never finished better than she did today. She's off to Wetherby now (for the Listed Wensleydale Juvenile Hurdle on November 3rd) and then she'll have a break. I can see her going back on the Flat in March before going back over hurdles and then we're thinking about maybe going over fences. She's a horse with a massive amount of upside and a lot of options - Ollie Pears, trainer.

quotes.race: Lee Pollard 60th Birthday Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: That's a big chunk of an allowance to get [18lb]. Max Of Stars does have Flat speed but couldn't win on the Flat because she was too keen. She was keen today as well but enjoys jumping and is big for a 3yo filly. At Newton Abbot she ran away with me and had everything burned off by halfway. To be fair to them the opposition is fairly exposed today and she was the unexposed one - Brian Hughes, rider, speaking to RacingTV.

runners.comment: C&D winner; ended long losing run in July; soft going suits; further rain a bonus; chance
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on soft in April 2023; ended a long losing run when scoring at Hamilton two runs ago (1m4f, good) and decent fourth over 1m5f on same track last time; further rain a bonus; chance.

runners.comment: Off the mark on 18th attempt in first-time blinkers last month; cheekpieces back on here
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her 18th Flat run when scoring in first-time blinkers at Musselburgh last month (1m5f, good); fair run when third in same headgear at Hamilton last time (1m4f, good); cheekpieces back on here.

runners.comment: 0-18; tailed off on last two starts following spell in France; hard to fancy
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden; had a spell in France in the spring (poor form); tailed off on both starts since returning to Phil Kirby; hard to fancy.

runners.owner: S Rimmer, L Taylor, Mrs R Carr
runners.comment: 2-27; 1m2f Redcar winner in May; not so good since; can pull hard; stamina to prove
runners.spotlight: Not the most prolific of winners (2-27); winner over 1m2f at Redcar in May (1m2f, good to firm; 18-1); hasn't run as well since and was well beaten on the AW on Thursday (1m2f); can pull hard and return to 1m4f not sure to suit.

stable_tour.quote: He came here from Karl Burke's over the summer and I have to say he's been disappointing. The silver lining is that his handicap rating has dropped to 60, which could put him in with a chance at Newcastle on Thursday. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Has won over 1m and 9.5f (AW) this year; stays 1m3f but below her best recently
runners.spotlight: Has won at Pontefract (1m, good) and Wolverhampton (9.5f) this season; not been in such good form recently; bit to prove now.

runners.comment: Maiden; tailed off latest (not best of runs); two fair runs before that; place chance
runners.spotlight: Maiden; tailed off at Southwell last time (1m6f; not the best of runs) but two fair runs over 1m6f at Yarmouth (good) and over 1m4f at Wolverhampton prior to that; place chance.

runners.comment: 0-13; in good form having finished runner-up last twice; headgear back on; place chance
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; comes here in good form having finished runner-up over 1m4f/1m2f at Ffos Las on last two starts (good/soft); cheekpieces on for second time (fair run previous occasion he wore them); each-way chance.

runners.comment: Maiden; in good form having been placed on last three starts; a possible
runners.spotlight: Maiden; comes here in good form having been placed on last three starts, notably when runner-up at Hamilton (1m4f, good; Lady Phoebe third) two runs back; a possible.

racecards.verdict: In a tricky handicap SUGARPIEHONEYBUNCH is taken to win her second race of the season, with any further rain a bonus.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Beaune, 7/2 Hickton, 4/1 Lady Phoebe, 11/2 Sugarpiehoneybunch, 6/1 Captious Cantor, 8/1 Max Of Stars, 16/1 Anificas Beauty, 25/1 Wait And Hope, 40/1 Yurinov

racecards.race_name: Patrick Hibbert-Foy Memorial Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Promise on first/third starts but she's not obviously well treated ahead of nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Promise on her first and third starts; not obviously well treated ahead of her nursery debut and ground slower than good to firm will be a new experience.

runners.comment: Promise in AW sprint novice events; bred to do better and she's an interesting contender
runners.spotlight: Gradual improvement in each of her three runs in fillies' novice events on AW (6f and 5f twice); bred to do much better at some point and she is an interesting contender on her turf/nursery debut.

runners.comment: Two promising course runs in the spring; stiff task latest; may do better now handicapping
runners.spotlight: Stiff task at York latest but her two course runs in the spring were encouraging; needs more now switched to nurseries but it's not out of the question.

runners.comment: Two improved efforts in nurseries, 2nd off this mark at Carlisle last week; solid claims
runners.spotlight: Improved for nurseries, winning a 5f event at Beverley last month and then a close second at Carlisle last week (6f, good to soft); solid claims off the same mark.

runners.comment: Not seen to best effect on recent nursery debut; still has time to do better
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to nurseries at Chelmsford last month, but he wasn't seen to best effect and remains capable of better; unraced on slower than good but he's one to note in the betting.

runners.comment: Not progressed in nurseries and latest Brighton third was just a modest one; vulnerable
runners.spotlight: She isn't progressing and latest Brighton third was a well-beaten one; has run well here in the past but looks vulnerable to anything on the up.

runners.comment: Not achieved much so far but looks a likely improver now handicapping in a tongue-tie
runners.spotlight: Finished no closer than 8l to the winner in his three runs in novice company (6f/7f; good or faster); sent off the 6-4 favourite for his second run, however, and looks a likely improver now handicapping in a tongue-tie and having been gelded.

runners.comment: Big prices and modest form in three maiden/novice runs; big step forward required
runners.spotlight: Big prices and modest form in three runs in maiden/novice company; should do better at some point but others have achieved more.

runners.comment: Yet to build on debut promise; gelded prior to nursery debut; ground a query
runners.spotlight: His debut effort (5.5f, good to firm) wasn't without hope but he hasn't built on it twice since; gelded prior to his nursery debut; may do better but still to prove he wants it slower than good.

runners.comment: Easy win at Catterick last week (6f, good); held by Run Therapy on an earlier Beverley run
runners.spotlight: Had been knocking at the door prior to her ready win at Catterick last week (6f, good); held by Rum Therapy on earlier Beverley form though.

runners.comment: Best effort so far when 3rd of ten at Redcar last month; cheekpieces added for h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on her first two starts (7f) but she shaped better when a front-running third of ten at Redcar three weeks ago (6f, good to firm); needs another step forward now handicapping but cheekpieces go on and she still has a bit of potential.

runners.comment: Not looked ahead of his mark in two nurseries so far; others bring more pressing claims
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise on both turf and AW but he hasn't suggested he was especially well handicapped; the likely slower ground needs to give him a lift.

runners.comment: Stayed on over 5f on last week's nursery debut (5f); looked a tricky ride there though
runners.spotlight: 5f looked inadequate on her nursery debut last week (AW) and she looked far-from-straightforward under pressure; that said, she was keeping on late and going back up in distance looks the right move.

racecards.verdict: Several possible improvers but RUM THERAPY has improved for nurseries and can confirm Beverley placings with Maasai Mata.
racecards.betting_forecast: 100/30 Maasai Mata, 4/1 Rum Therapy, 6/1 King Victorious, 8/1 Perfect Panda,  Brain Freeze, 9/1 Walk On Walk On, 12/1 Double Naughty, 16/1 Hellcat,  Giles Glory,  Grey Horizon,  Mademoiselle Belle, 25/1 Filly Foden,  Daizen

racecards.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (Sire And Dam Restricted Race) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: 16-1 when never featuring in a C&D novice last month; likely a longer-term prospect
runners.spotlight: 16-1 when never featuring in a C&D novice last month (good to firm); likely one for the longer term.

runners.comment: Similar form in two runs, the latest over C&D last month; can do better but needs to
runners.spotlight: Similar form in two runs this summer, latterly when keeping on for fifth of 13 over C&D last month (good to firm); more to come but such progress will likely be needed; stable also runs North Force.

runners.comment: Fine pedigree and shaped well over 6f on debut; open to plenty of improvement
runners.spotlight: By Frankel out of a Group 2 winner; kept on steadily for third on his Doncaster debut in July (6f, good) and should be well suited by today's extra furlong; leading contender.

runners.comment: Well held on debut in May (7f, good to soft) but only 6-1 there and bred to do much better
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling with an attractive pedigree; however, he finished last of six on debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft; 6-1) in May and needs to leave that well behind.

runners.comment: 70,000gns half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Biniorella Bay (RPR 89); stable 31% with 2yos here
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; second foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Biniorella Bay (RPR 89); dam 7f AW winner (84); likely type on paper and powerful stable has a good record (31%) with the 2yos they send to Thirsk.

runners.comment: Modest form in two 7f maidens this summer; not easily recommended
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two 7f maidens this summer; not easily recommended.

runners.comment: Finished 3rd in both starts, failing to improve from his debut; still has time on his side
runners.spotlight: Promising Epsom debut in July but he failed to build on it at Beverley last month (7f/7.4f; both good to firm); still early days but he'll need more for the likely slower ground.

runners.comment: No impact in two runs last month but neither was without promise; each-way shout
runners.spotlight: No impact in two runs over further last month; showed enough to think he had a future though and minor money not out of the question.

runners.comment: 80,000euros half-brother to 14.5f winner Clockmaker (RPR 86); dam useful; check betting
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns foal, 80,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 14.5f winner Clockmaker (RPR 86); dam Listed-placed 7f-1m2f winner (102); likely type on paper and he's a newcomer of note.

racecards.verdict: Richard Fahey may hold the key here with NORTH FORCE, who shaped well over 6f on debut, preferred to Kiss For An Angel.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 North Force, 7/2 Poetry Of Time, 4/1 Sovereign Wealth, 6/1 Take A Tip, 8/1 Kiss For An Angel, 10/1 On The Inlet, 14/1 Stoneacre Donny, 20/1 Bradbury, 50/1 Secret Testimony

racecards.race_name: Sapphire Selling Handicap

runners.comment: Bolted up (well backed) on last week's handicap debut (6f, AW); well in under a penalty
runners.spotlight: Had a wind operation prior to last week's handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) and it produced a transformation, rewarding strong market support in emphatic fashion, eased down inside the final 100yds and still winning by over 5l; clearly well in under a penalty and although he has to prove as good on turf, and the fact he's dropped into a selling handicap is disconcerting, he's hard to oppose.

runners.comment: No impact in two handicaps this summer; drop in class needs to spark a full revival
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden company but no impact in two handicaps this summer; drops in class and Billy Loughnane takes over for the first time.

runners.comment: Solid stable debut at Ffos Las last month; makes handicap debut at a realistic level
runners.spotlight: Didn't build on her debut promise in two further runs for Clive Cox; second of seven on her stable debut at Ffos Las last month (6f, good; cheekpieces on for the first time); makes her handicap debut at a realistic level and could have more to come.

runners.comment: On a losing run but latest effort more encouraging; down in class on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his old form but 2024 was a struggle and it's not been much better in 2025; latest Ripon fifth was more encouraging though and he drops in class on his stable debut (same owner); has shown enough on slow ground to think rain wouldn't be an issue.

quotes.race: Clean Up With PPS Handicap
quotes.quote: The Green Man did that easily enough and he's in good order with himself, he hasn't moved as well as this in a long time. He's flourishing. He's a big horse and he's just fully maturing - Joseph Parr, trainer.

runners.comment: On a good mark, won't mind rain and new headgear could help; one to take seriously
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins at Doncaster last summer (soft and good); not found her best in three runs this summer but won't mind rain and she drops in class with cheekpieces given a go; one to take seriously.

runners.comment: Tumbled down weights; latest effort offered hope of better; may have found his level now
runners.spotlight: Began the year with a mark 24lb higher but it's been a struggle in handicaps, latterly for new yard; latest effort, when running into dead end after dead end in the straight at Haydock (7f, good; blinkers first time) was a bit more encouraging and he may have found his level now.

runners.comment: All wins on good to firm but handles slower; others look more persuasive this time
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come over 6f on good to firm ground, the latest off 1lb lower in June; effective on good to soft, as when third of ten at Hamilton 12 days ago, but others appeal more for win purposes.

quotes.race: Racing TV Handicap
quotes.quote: I had to be patient today to get the gap, but once it opened One Of Our Own picked up well and put her head down. I kept it simple, but she's an honest filly who tries. This year she hasn't been running too bad. She got a bit unlucky at Redcar, she's run well and been placed a few times. It's nice to get her head in front today - Kaiya Fraser, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Won two 7f classified events last month; runner-up last two completed starts; e-w shout
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 7f classified events last month; runner-up in his last two completed starts; unseated rider early at Chester on Friday; each-way shout but hardly an unknown quantity.

stable_tour.quote: He's a fun horse who shows a liking for Carlisle. He'd been working well before his win there last month, when he was badly drawn but they went off quick and came back to him. He's run all right since but things haven't really worked out for him, he needs a good pace. He'll win more at his level. 26-07-24

runners.comment: On her winning mark but it has been tough going for her so far in 2025
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f nursery at Beverley off this mark last August; struggled so far in 2025 though and others appeal more under these conditions.

runners.comment: Inconsistent 12-race maiden; drop back to 6f not enough to tempt
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 12-race maiden; not beaten far at Musselburgh (7f; sent off joint favourite) last time but even that effort needs bettering here.

runners.comment: Solid in-frame efforts the last twice; in the mix with a repeat
runners.spotlight: Solid in-frame efforts at Ayr and Southwell on her last two starts; shouldn't be far away with a repeat.

runners.comment: Return to sprinting might help but she will need to raise her game a level to gain 1st win
runners.spotlight: In the money in three runs in July/August (7f-1m; various ground) but she's dropped away having gone off hard on her last two starts; return to sprinting might help but others appeal more.

runners.comment: Winning stable debut in June (7f, AW) but little to shout about since
runners.spotlight: Made a winning stable debut at Newcastle in June (7f) but not much to shout about since; too much to prove for comfort.

racecards.verdict: He has a different surface to contend with but HOCKNEY looked miles in front of the handicapper when winning at Lingfield last week.
racecards.betting_forecast: 10/11 Hockney, 8/1 B Associates, 10/1 Speaktotheguvnor,  The Green Man, 12/1 Line Of Force,  Laurens Dream, 14/1 One Of Our Own, 16/1 Tuscan Point, 20/1 Dazy Mazy,  Tootsie, 25/1 Ottawa,  Go On Rosie, 33/1 Almutraf

racecards.race_name: decronhorsecare.co.uk Handicap

runners.comment: Still to win after nine starts but she's regularly threatened; in the mix once again
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; 0-9 but she has been knocking at the door all season and was beaten just half a length into third at Haydock ten days ago; nudged up 1lb but should give it another good go.

runners.comment: 0-7 in novice/maiden company and ran poorly latest; fair mark for handicap debut though
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in seven runs in novice/maiden company and her form nosedived at Southwell 11 days ago; this should be more suitable though and she's not dismissed.

runners.comment: First past post in both starts in 2025 (1m); still feasibly treated and 7f should be fine
runners.spotlight: Won in spite of racing freely on her seasonal return in August (1m, good); first past the post at Salisbury 17 days ago but subsequently demoted after interfering with the second horse; another small rise in the weights shouldn't prevent a big run.

runners.comment: 6f AW wins as a 2yo; no progress since and still to prove his stamina for 7f
runners.spotlight: Three promising efforts as a 2yo, including a 6f win at Newcastle; the handicapper has been in command this season and he didn't convince with his stamina when upped to 7f at Southwell three weeks ago.

runners.comment: 7f AW winner; solid second at Ayr last month but she'll need more to win this
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden win on AW in June; only midfield in her first handicap but she fared better when second of eight at Ayr five weeks ago (7f, good); capable of better but such progress will be needed in this competitive event.

prev_owners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton

runners.comment: Regressed for Richard Fahey; pulled up on hurdle/stable debut three weeks ago; opposable
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden at Hamilton on his 2yo debut but he struggled in five further runs for Richard Fahey; 66-1 when pulled up on his hurdle/stable debut at Cartmel three weeks ago; plenty to prove.

runners.comment: Two 7f turf wins this summer; near miss on AW latest; high on the list
runners.spotlight: Two easy 7f wins in May/June; revised mark hasn't looked beyond him and he went close at Kempton four weeks ago (7f, AW); no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well once more.

runners.comment: 3rd of 5 at Salisbury (1m, good to soft) last time; drop to 7f can help; not fully exposed
runners.spotlight: Only five ran when he finished third at Salisbury 17 days ago (1m, good to soft) but it still represented improvement; drop in trip can help and he's not fully exposed.

runners.comment: Nine-race maiden; not kicked on from a promising reappearance; stamina for 7f not assured
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; went close over 6f (good to soft) at Ripon on his reappearance in May but he hasn't built on it in three runs since; stamina for 7f still to be proven.

runners.breeder: Al Shaqab Racing & S C E A Des Prairies
runners.comment: Led late when scoring at Carlisle five weeks ago (7f, good); contender up just 3lb
runners.spotlight: Failed to justify market support over 6f here last month but he came from off the pace to lead late in a 7f handicap at Carlisle four days later; 3lb rise not excessive and more appealing than many.

runners.comment: 0-7 but runner-up three times, including on Thursday (7f, AW); more to prove on turf
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she has threatened on occasions, including when a close second at Newcastle on Thursday (7f); best turf run came here (1m) but on good to firm; rain would be a query.

stable_tour.quote: She's a big filly who has wintered well. She finished second on her debut at Newcastle in October and then was third at Ripon last week over 6f. She'll probably be seen to best effect up in trip but we're just trying to get her to sharpen up and race into the bridle at the moment. I think she'll be a nice filly down the line. 23-04-25

runners.comment: Little solid form and latest 3rd came in a slowly run race; improvement a must
runners.spotlight: Little solid form and her latest third of five came in a slowly run affair; big step forward needed now handicapping.

runners.comment: Handles soft ground well; good 2nd on penultimate start but couldn't back it up last week
runners.spotlight: Two wins on soft ground last year; having dropped in the weights he ran well for second at Musselburgh last month (7f, good); backwards step last time but the more rain to fall the better.

racecards.verdict: Newmarket raiders Mamma Maria and ME TARZAN can fight this out with James Fanshawe's runner marginally preferred.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Mamma Maria, 4/1 Me Tarzan, 7/1 Highfield Sunshine, 8/1 Sir David,  Miss Yorkshire, 10/1 Empire Power,  Call Me Betty, 12/1 Draupnir,  Law Degree, 14/1 On Key, 25/1 Mu Mu Land, 33/1 Urban Sky,  Siviez

racecards.race_name: Geoff Scott 70th Birthday Celebration Fillies' Handicap

runners.comment: Yet to shine in handicaps; new headgear given a go; down in the weights but opposable
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Nottingham last summer (8.3f, good to firm) but 0-9 in handicaps since; low-key return from a 12-week break this month and cheekpieces are now given a go; others appeal more.

runners.comment: Cheekpieces have had a positive effect; can't be ruled out in current form
runners.spotlight: The addition of cheekpieces has had a positive effect and she has two wins and three seconds from five starts since the aids went on; should give her running once again.

runners.owner: W H & Mrs J A Tinning & Mrs C Wallis
runners.comment: 1m win on soft ground in June; well held twice since; vulnerable to the 3yos
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to win a 1m handicap at Carlisle (soft) in June; not so good twice since and vulnerable to the 3yos this time.

runners.owner: J P L Flavour Technologies Ltd
runners.comment: Flopped at Haydock last time; new headgear today and unproven on slower than good
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 1m handicap at Haydock in May (good to firm); respectable efforts on her next two starts but she stopped quickly when tailed off at Haydock last month (cheekpieces first time); something to prove now tried in a visor; unraced on slower than good.

stable_tour.quote: She was no better than fifth in three runs last year, but she was really weak then and has strengthened up now. She's been given an opening mark of 67 and I'd be hopeful she's better than that. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Similar form in three novice/maiden runs; no surprise to see marked improvement today
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form in three novice/maiden runs (7f-8.3.f); needs improvement to make winning handicap debut but her pedigree strongly suggests there should be more to come and she represents a top stable; interesting.

runners.comment: 1m win on soft ground in June; midfield on AW latest but still unexposed on turf
runners.spotlight: Good start for new stable when third of nine at Kempton (1m) in May; ready win at Ffos Las the following month (1m, soft) and although she was only midfield at Kempton five weeks ago, she could resume her progress back on turf; rain would help.

runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn, A Dick And P Snook
runners.comment: Steps up in trip after a wind op and has shown promise; not fully exposed; contender
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise, including here, and has undergone wind surgery since finishing in midfield behind Pearly Squirrel at Doncaster in July; some encouragement for a mile on pedigree and she's not fully exposed.

runners.comment: Four AW wins this year but she's up in the weights and has much more to prove on turf
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW this year (1m-1m3f); came up short off this mark at Southwell three weeks ago and she has something to prove on turf.

runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

prev_owners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow
Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.comment: Of interest on best form and the returning tongue-tie can help; rain would be a worry
runners.spotlight: The addition of cheekpieces saw her get off the mark in a 7f handicap at Doncaster in July (good to firm); stays 1m but she wasn't at her best at Southwell 11 days ago; the return of the tongue-tie can help but her chance would likely be compromised by rain.

runners.comment: Failed to reward market support on stable debut last month; blinkers return; low mileage
runners.spotlight: Strong in the betting ahead of her stable debut at Catterick but she could only manage fifth of the 12 runners (7f, good); the return of the blinkers worn in Ireland this summer might help.

runners.comment: Tailed off at 100-1 on last month's stable/turf debut; too much to prove
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress for Richard Hughes and was tailed off at 100-1 on last month's stable/turf debut at Catterick; need to see more before getting involved.

racecards.verdict: Bintshuaa and Velvet Whisper can do better but so too can DAY OF GRACE (nap) and she appeals as a likely big improver.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Day Of Grace, 4/1 Ravishing Beauty,  Bintshuaa, 11/2 Northern Attitude, 7/1 Velvet Whisper, 12/1 Pearly Squirrel, 14/1 Pasha,  Catton Lady,  Adorla Of Achill, 16/1 Eva Rosie, 50/1 Pink Petunia

racecards.race_name: Bowel Cancer Screening Programme Saves Lives Handicap

runners.comment: Well handicapped now and took good step back in right direction when fourth at Doncaster
runners.spotlight: Not firing on all cylinders this season, but well handicapped now and took good step in right direction when fourth at Doncaster (7f, good) in July; eased in grade and well drawn; forecast rain a worry.

quotes.race: Kimberly Wyatt @ Scottish Sun Ladies Night Handicap
quotes.quote: They went very hard and I thought the pace was always going to collapse and it helped the closers like me. Al Muqdad has been very consistent and good, quicker ground is probably his thing. Seven furlongs is probably his best trip - Danny Tudhope, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: May low-key stable debut last week but that came after a layoff; lurks on a tempting mark
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield over C&D on recent stable debut, but that was his first run for six months and he's on a tempting mark now; drawn wide but won't mind what the weather does; a possible.

stable_tour.quote: He had a wind operation at the back end of last year and really thrived. We've trained quite a few of the family and they get better with age. His win first time back at Redcar showed he's on the up so we're expecting him to go on to better things, probably at a mile. 03-05-23

runners.comment: Went close on last month's stable debut and subsequent defeat not viewed too harshly
runners.spotlight: Gained all three wins over 6f on AW but has form on turf and stays at least 1m; went close on last month's stable debut (6f, AW) and subsequent defeat in a competitive race is not viewed too harshly; not discounted.

runners.comment: Yet to strike form for new stable this year but well worth a glance in the betting
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; yet to strike form after four runs for new stable this season but will cope if the ground softens and is quite well drawn; Billy Loughnane rides; well worth a second look.

stable_tour.quote: Another by Inns Of Court, he will be ready around July time. He is a really useful horse and is a half-brother to the owner's Persian Force, winner of last year's Brocklesby. 10-05-23

runners.comment: Down the field this month, after a break, but heavy rain will increase his appeal
runners.spotlight: Won narrowly off his lower AW mark in March; returned from layoff with down-the-field AW run this month but combination of refitted cheekpieces and heavy rain could prompt a more dynanic performance.

quotes.race: Deepbridge Estate Planning Service Handicap
quotes.quote: We didn't know about the trip and if Broken Spear would stay or not but we went a nice gallop and he's just picked them off one by one. He feels like a seven furlong horse all day long and he fights as well because the others weren't going away and he just stuck his head down - Kieran Schofield, jockey.

quotes.race: British EBF Supporting Racing To School Novice Stakes (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: We gave Broken Spear a mid-season break because the ground was too firm - he wants it good to soft. He's a tough and straightforward horse who is all speed. That's him finished for the season - Tony Coyle, trainer.

runners.comment: Too well handicapped to rule out but has questions to answer after two tame efforts
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2024; shaped as though ready to exploit his reduced mark at Catterick (7f, good to soft) in July but two tame efforts have followed and he now has questions to answer again.

prev_owners.owner: Jastar Ltd, Murt Khan & Harry Wigan

runners.comment: In good form this season but moves up in grade today; others look better handicapped
runners.spotlight: As good as even during three-race campaign this season, most notably when winning at Ayr (1m, good to soft) on penultimate outing; also suited by 7f but moves up in grade and others look better handicapped.

runners.comment: Has remained in good form since narrow C&D win in July but is drawn wide
runners.spotlight: Did just enough to score over C&D (good) in July and has remained in good form since; lost out only by a nose when upped to 1m at Ripon recently but holds no secrets from the handicapper and is drawn wide.

prev_owners.owner: D Fravigar, S Jones And Partner

runners.comment: Just held on at Doncaster last month but his tendency to start slowly is always a worry
runners.spotlight: Just held on to score at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month and ran at least as well when close second on AW 16 days ago, but his tendency to start slowly is always a worry and rain probably won't help him.

runners.comment: Has mixed record this year but significant rain will enhance his appeal
runners.spotlight: Won three times on soft ground at Catterick (7f) last year; has mixed record this season and was soundly beaten last time, but he's on a good mark now and significant rain will enhance his appeal.

runners.comment: Went close in modest race two starts ago but others have more pressing claims
runners.spotlight: Seen only four times this year, with best run his close second of six in modest race at Carlisle (7f, good to soft) two starts ago; sports refitted tongue-tie/cheekpieces; others have more pressing claims.

runners.owner: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr
runners.comment: Has earned his keep in low-level handicaps this season; might find this too competitive
runners.spotlight: Has earned his keep in low-level handicaps this season, winning over C&D and twice over 7.4f at Beverley (all on good to firm); also suited by soft ground but might find this race too competitive.

stable_tour.quote: He was a winner of a Southwell maiden for Marcus Tregoning and he ran well back there for us first time this season, where he wasn't beaten too far. He's a miler.
