I can't find anything for him back at Southwell, so I'm hoping the ground has dried up a bit for him at Pontefract on April 22. 17-04-24

runners.comment: Dual winner at regular haunt Beverley this year; on tough mark now and opposable here
runners.spotlight: Seen to good effect on stiff, right-handed tracks and has collected two Beverley wins this season (7.4f/8.4f, good to firm); on a fairly tough mark now, though, and looks opposable from stall 11.

racecards.verdict: Not been seen to best effect in AW sprints this summer, MANHATTAN MIRAGE is well handicapped now and this test looks more suitable.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Al Muqdad, 13/2 Wilde And Dandy, 7/1 Reidh,  Travis, 8/1 Ey Up Its Jazz, 10/1 Homer Stokes, 12/1 The Cookstown Cafu, 14/1 Manhattan Mirage,  Broken Spear,  Naval Academy,  Dumfries, 20/1 Sunny Orange

racecards.race_name: BetWright Bet The Wright Way Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.comment: Very useful hurdler; easily beat two modest rivals on chasing debut in May; respected
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler who looked as good as ever when scoring by over 5l at Warwick (3m1f, good) in April then easily beat two modest rivals in 2m4f beginners' chase there the following month; may not be ideally suited by this drop back to 2m but still commands respect.

quotes.race: From The Horses Mouth Podcast Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was far enough back but we hadn't gone that quick and he jumped on the bridle straight away and put the race to bed quickly. You wouldn't know how good Doyen Quest is as he keeps a bit back for himself - Harry Skelton, jockey, talking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Kingston Stud Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was lovely. Doyen Quest was probably going to improve for a step up in trip and he likes a bit of better ground. A fast-ish run race round here suits him and he doesn't do a lot when he gets to the front so a lovely ride from Harry [Skelton] - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won decent handicaps at Newbury and Cheltenham in the spring and put up a very good performance when second in the Silver Trophy at Chepstow on his reappearance, beaten by a fitter horse on the day. I'd say he'll be progressive this year, especially over a little bit further - the step up to three miles should bring about improvement. I'd love to go to the November meeting at Cheltenham with him but the ground will dictate plans. 12-11-24

runners.comment: 4-8 over hurdles, and easy Irish point win in 2022 bodes well for her chasing prospects
runners.spotlight: 4-8 over hurdles; ran well in defeat off today's mark at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) on latest outing ten weeks ago, and her easy Irish point win in 2022 bodes very well for her chasing prospects.

quotes.race: Visit Irish Store Sales With ITM Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Seeyouinmydreams is a fair horse and she'll go to Aintree now for the mares' bumper there. Harry had never sat on her, but said he thought she'd win going to the start because she felt so good. We haven't pushed many buttons and we thought she was nice, but you never know until you run them. She's already won a point-to-point, so will be a lovely hurdler next season after a summer of strengthening - Paul Nicholls, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's a lovely prospect and I've been delighted with her, but we're in it for the long game with fillies like her and I'm in no rush. She was very impressive when hacking up by 15 lengths in a Irish mares' maiden point-to-point at Largy in April, after which she was bought at the Tattersalls May NH Sale. She didn't come cheap but all the right faces were trying to buy her. She's from a great family, being related to three winners, notably the smart hurdler Songe. Two or three runs in bumpers will suffice for this season, but without doubt she's a name to remember for the future. 31-10-22

runners.comment: Three wins this summer, the latest when back over fences at Newton Abbot; 2lb wrong here
runners.spotlight: Returned from layoff with two hurdle wins in July (2m2f/2m) and was too good for progressive stablemate of Seeyouinmydreams when back over fences at Newton Abbot (2m, good) last month; effectively 5lb higher from 2lb out of the weights here but still taken very seriously.

quotes.race: John Price Memorial Chase Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Breaking Cover has form on slower ground, which made me fancy him even more before the race. He was big at a handful of jumps and very clever at the last - Sam Twiston-Davies, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He's a good-quality horse and seems to be having a bit more of a clear run - the wheels fell off a bit after his first run last season, although he still managed to come back and finish third in a maiden hurdle. He's speed not stamina and we'll try to find a two-mile novice hurdle. 12-10-23

runners.comment: 2-6 over hurdles; could have a future over fences but is 8lb wrong here
runners.spotlight: 2-6 over hurdles last season, the wins gained over 2m6f and 2m5f; could have a future over fences and, given her tendency to race freely, this drop back in trip might work for her; however, she has fitness to prove after a six-month absence and is 8lb out of the weights.

quotes.race: racingtv.com/freetrial Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Springs A Girl should've never been that price. We bought her after winning a bumper in Ireland and really liked her run at Cheltenham, when she stayed up the hill strongly to be midfield. My horses were sick when she ran at Catterick so we put a line through that. She hit the line strongly and there's more to come - Martin Keighley, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Doyen Quest is the class act in the line-up but chasing debutante SEEYOUINMYDREAMS may have more potential over fences.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Doyen Quest,  Seeyouinmydreams, 11/4 Breaking Cover, 16/1 Springs A Girl

racecards.race_name: Paul Ferguson's Jumpers To Follow Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.comment: Good strike-rate over fences and able when fresh; Harry Skelton rides Snipe
runners.spotlight: Enhanced his good strike-rate over fences in a Class 2 contest at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) in April and reportedly made a noise when stopping quickly next time; can run well when fresh but Harry Skelton rides Snipe.

quotes.race: Get Best Odds Guaranteed At BetVictor Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Heltenham won over hurdles at the start of the season and maybe had a hard race, but he definitely had a hard race at Aintree after that on bad ground. Before Kempton in February, his coat massively changed and I knew he hadn't been right. He's come into form and is pretty effective round here and we'll look at all sorts of races. Maybe Sandown and the Oaksey Chase will suit him at the end of the season, a big galloping track. If a Bravemansgame turns up in that, he isn't in his league, but there's a race at Perth for him and options at Ayr and he's in the Topham too - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: Dransfields CIU Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Heltenham was back over hurdles and it was a bit different for him. His jumping has never been his forte, back over hurdles he was knocking them but he's gone and done the job and that's the main thing - Tristan Durrell, jockey.

quotes.race: Run For Your Money At BetVictor Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Heltenham is improving all the time and hopefully keeps improving. Chasing has helped him but time more than anything. I could see him one day coming here for a Coral Gold Cup, but that might be in 2024 rather than the autumn. More immediately, he could go to Sandown on the last day of the season for a novice handicap chase, but it would have to keep raining. He wants really slow ground, it's very important to him - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: The ground hasn't been soft enough for him yet this season but we're aiming to go to Newbury with him for a two-and-a-half-mile handicap chase at Newbury on November 29. We thought he was going the right way last term and he won a £100,000 Grade 3 handicap at Newbury in the spring - more of the same will be on the agenda. 12-11-24

runners.breeder: S A R L Phoenix Eventing
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton
runners.comment: Wouldn't appreciate any recent rain but won well off 4lb lower at Aintree in May
runners.spotlight: Wouldn't appreciate any recent rain but won well off 4lb lower at Aintree in May (3m1f, good) and effectively 3lb lower here with Harry Atkins taking off 7lb; may need that trip these days.

quotes.race: CPMS Novices' Champion Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We won this three years ago with Molly The Dolly and we've had it in mind we wanted to come back for it with Sail Away. I had him entered for the Scottish National but that may be next year. He loves nice ground, his jumping counted for a lot and he was a fresh horse. He'll probably go for the Summer Cup at Uttoxeter unless the ground is particularly quick - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's won some decent races but he had a crashing fall at Newbury last week, having shown plenty of enthusiasm beforehand. His chances will come when the handicapper gives him a break, over two and a half to three miles on decent ground. 12-11-24

runners.comment: Can be hard to predict but she's capable of a big run; ground too fast last time out
runners.spotlight: Reverted to fences with a hard-fought success at Stratford (2m1f, good) and perhaps found good to firm too lively when tailed off back there 28 days ago; can be hard to predict but she's capable of a big run.

quotes.race: Skip Hire Flyer Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We went a good gallop, which suited Arclight. She felt the last couple of times like she'd been lacking a bit of toe, so a strongly run two miles over fences really suited her and she jumped really well. Her last run over fences didn't go to plan and she lost a bit of confidence, and that's the first time she's run over them since. She obviously enjoys it and a few more jumps in the way probably helped her - James Bowen, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Trustatrader Fully Vetted Tradespeople Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: All credit to Nico, it was his idea [to go chasing] and Arclight was very good. She had all the allowances and it's why you need a good jockey like that who is thinking. We've got to do the same thing next time because you've still got all the allowances; she's always going to be a mare, but she's not always going to be a four-year-old. Walking out of the saddling box I said she should be going down to the starting stalls, not jumping fences because she's a Flat-bred, but she was on springs. Until the last, she was excellent. She needs that better ground and it's beautiful ground for these types - Nicky Henderson, trainer.

quotes.race: Play Coral "Racing-Super-Series" For Free Fillies' Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Arclight is a sweet filly and, as Nico says, she is very game and genuine. She handled that but I can't believe she liked it. There is a four year old fillies' Listed race at Doncaster on Friday week. I think now that if all was right we go there to get a bit of black type, then it's a different ball game altogether - winning trainer Nicky Henderson.

quotes.race: bet365 Fillies' Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (Go North Grey Bomber Juvenile Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB)
quotes.quote: We're really pleased with that. Arclight will learn from that, we'll give her another run and the Boodles is a possibility if she's good enough. But she's very straightforward and fingers crossed we've got something decent - syndicate leader, David Sumpter.

stable_tour.quote: She's been a little star and has grown and strengthened up considerably over the summer. She made a good start over hurdles and it was Nico's [de Boinville] idea to try her over fences last season. I thought it was a brave shout, but she was absolutely brilliant and won three of her four starts. If it wasn't for an injury she would have been out in the spring, but we look to resume over fences and she has a very bright future. 22-10-24

runners.comment: Chased home a well-handicapped Irish raider in the Summer Plate at Market Rasen
runners.spotlight: Record of 7-17 over fences; second run back from an absence when chasing home a well-handicapped Irish raider in the Summer Plate at Market Rasen two months ago (2m5f); should give another good account.

quotes.race: Dora Larner 92nd Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: He's been out of form for a little while but dropped to a nice mark today. Courtland had a year off and took ages to come back but today he looked really well and he felt better, he travelled better, and won nicely - Sean Bowen, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: We've loved Courtland from day one - he's a lovely, kind horse, so laidback and has a great attitude. We bought him as a foal in Ireland so to see him grow up and then go on to win races is very rewarding. His form has been well franked and hopefully he'll make into a nice chaser - Gwyn Brace, part-owner.

stable_tour.quote: He had an injury after his last race, the bet365 Gold Cup at Sandown last April, but he's back in work and has done well. He'll probably have one run and then go straight to the Summer Plate at Market Rasen in July, a race in which he finished a very close second in 2023. 26-03-25

runners.comment: Hunter chasing when last seen (running well) and returns to handicaps off a fair mark
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner who was second in the hunter chase at Aintree's Grand National meeting in April; returns to handicaps off a handy mark.

quotes.race: Central Roofing Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I thought we could win, I'm not going to lie. We learnt at Worcester Jet Plane wanted a proper trip for the first time. Bridget [Andrews] rode him and he couldn't be any further forwards but he couldn't go any faster so we knew he wanted three miles and here we are. This was worth 18,200GBP, if they keep putting that money up then we'll keep targeting these races - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a highly consistent horse and ran really well when placed at Wetherby on his reappearance. He has a great attitude and all he does is try his best, so I'd like to win a good one with him. A race like the Rowland Meyrick, back at Wetherby on Boxing Day, might be ideal. 12-11-24

runners.comment: Posted a useful RPR in winning latest hunter chase so retains plenty of ability
runners.spotlight: Twice behind stablemate Jet Plane in hunter chases before winning comfortably from the front at Stratford (2m1f, good; stays 3m); posted a useful RPR last time so retains plenty of ability.

quotes.race: Coral Racing Club Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (For The Fulke Walwyn Trophy) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Frere d'Armes has got a fabulous attitude and jumping is obviously his game. He was impressive at Kempton last time. It's much better ground today but that didn't worry us as he's an accurate mover - I don't think he's a really soft ground horse.  It was a brilliant performance and I'll need a vet's opinion on a cut he has on his leg. I hope it's cosmetic and I'm not in a massive rush with him, so I don't think we want to ask him too many big questions too soon. I'd rather give him time and the cut will force that, but I don't think that's a  bad thing. Even if he didn't run until February I wouldn't mind and we'll see how the novice looks, but something like the Pendil could suit - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: EBF "National Hunt" Novices' Hurdle (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Frere D'Armes has done it well. He bumped into a nice one the last day. I couldn't have asked for any more from him and it's a good platform to build on - Harry Skelton, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He ran pretty well in a good handicap chase over two miles and one furlong at Ascot last time. He's been consistent but hasn't won for a while, although I'm sure it's coming his way. 12-11-24

runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing & T S Ingham
runners.comment: Runs well now and again but not last time and losing run is mounting up
runners.spotlight: Went close at Market Rasen (2m5f) in June but behind Courtland back there next time and has since disappointed at Stratford; losing run is mounting up.

quotes.race: BoyleSports Grand Sefton Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: The Topham Chase in the spring and this race again next year would be Vintage Fizz's targets if he gets his ground again. The Becher Chase would be too far for him so he'll have his holiday now and come back in the spring - Jedd O'Keeffe, trainer.

runners.comment: Competitive mark on last season's peak form but a longer distance would be preferred
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts in 3m chases last season before falling in the Scottish National; struggled back over hurdles when last seen but wouldn't read much into that; has no ground preferences and Harry Skelton rides (trainer runs five), but it's a long time since he ran over this sharp a trip.

quotes.race: William Hill Epic Boost Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Snipe is going the right way and could be one for marathon distances in the spring - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's run well on two trips to Carlisle this season, probably not staying the three and a quarter miles last time in quite a competitive race. There are definitely three-mile chases to be won with him. 12-11-24

runners.owner: The Risk And Reward Racing Syndicate
runners.comment: Latest third at Stratford was remote and he tends to save his best form for Huntingdon
runners.spotlight: Remote third on his return to fences at Stratford four weeks ago and tends to save his best form for Huntingdon.

runners.comment: Won off this mark for the Skeltons; well beaten in French hurdles for current yard
runners.spotlight: Last win for the Skeltons was off this mark last December (1m7f, soft) but he's offered little since, including in two French hurdles for current yard.

quotes.race: William Hill Epic Value Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Over two miles with a bit of cut in the ground, Mount Tempest is an improving horse. He likes to boss the race, hanging on to him is not for him at all, he wants to be in charge. Fergus Gillard gave him a super ride - Jason Fildes, northern racing manager of Highclere Thoroughbreds.

quotes.race: Best Odds On The Betfair Exchange Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Mount Tempest has obviously got plenty of ability but he's a little rascal. The other day at Wetherby we gave him a chance to get the trip and he just stayed behind. I think he'll stay two and a half miles. The trip isn't the biggest problem, just his application, but the blinkers have helped him. I hope he can give the syndicate a bit of fun. He'll probably go to the Winter Millions meeting for the novice handicap chase there - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won twice for us last season and loves it when the mud's flying. When he gets those conditions I'm sure he'll pick up the winning thread again. Soft ground and two miles are ideal for him, and we may have to go in search of those, although we could go a little further with him if necessary. 12-11-24

racecards.verdict: The 10yo COURTLAND (nap) bumped into a well-handicapped rival in the Summer Plate and he's the one to beat.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Courtland,  Snipe, 6/1 Heltenham,  Sail Away,  Arclight, 10/1 Jet Plane,  Vintage Fizz,  No Risk With Lou, 12/1 Frere d'Armes, 20/1 Mount Tempest

racecards.race_name: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.comment: 20-1 when trailing home last at Stratford, finishing behind Harbour Haze
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when trailing home last at Stratford, finishing behind Harbour Haze.

runners.comment: Flat-bred gelding who cost £5,500 as a 2yo; half-brother to a 1m4f winner
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred gelding who cost £5,500 as a 2yo; half-brother to a 1m4f winner but can only be watched on this belated debut.

runners.comment: Showed some ability when a well-held third on good to firm at Stratford last month
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability when a well-held third on good to firm at Stratford last month; probably has to improve on that.

runners.comment: Finished tailed off in a 3m good-ground point in May 2024; best watched
runners.spotlight: Finished tailed off in a 3m good-ground point in May 2024; best watched.

runners.breeder: Pierre Tarsieu De Maleissye Melun & Alex
runners.comment: Second at Market Rasen (good) three months ago behind a subsequent winner who made all
runners.spotlight: Second at Market Rasen (good) three months ago behind a subsequent winner who made all; left Tom George for £20,000 three weeks later; could be the one to beat.

runners.comment: £14,000 3yo; respected newcomer from a yard that wins plenty of bumpers
runners.spotlight: £14,000 3yo; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Rectory Road (7f/1m; RPR 90), Glorious Star (7f; 74) and Ensign Ewart (German 9.7f-12.5f); dam unraced half-sister to dual 1,000 Guineas third La Nuit Rose and useful 2m hurdler King Of Queens; respected newcomer from a yard that wins plenty of bumpers.

runners.comment: 15,000euros 3yo; nice pedigree and stable has plenty of success in bumpers
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros 3yo; half-brother to five winners including Magic Daze (smart 2m hurdle/chase), Magic Seven (bumper and 2m/2m4f hurdle) and Magic Day (2m3f hurdle); unraced dam out of half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Kicking King; nice pedigree and stable has plenty of success in bumpers.

runners.comment: 2,000euros 3yo; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Birchdale
runners.spotlight: 2,000euros 3yo; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Birchdale; best to let the market guide.

runners.comment: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Cher Tara; dam maiden in bumpers/over hurdles
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Cher Tara; dam maiden in bumpers/over hurdles, half-sister to 3m chase winner Book Of Gold; likely contender for powerful yard.

runners.comment: Half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Before Midnight
runners.spotlight: £15,000 3yo; half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Before Midnight; dam 3m hurdle winner; stable 1-18 in bumpers recent seasons but worth checking in the betting.

racecards.verdict: Rodeo appeals most of those with experience but LUCKY DIAMOND has a nice pedigree and may make a winning debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Rodeo, 3/1 Lucky Diamond, 7/2 Trigg, 4/1 Hopeless Dancer, 8/1 Harbour Haze, 14/1 Noonetellsmenothin, 25/1 All About Moi, 33/1 Four Decades, 50/1 Onehundredneighty, 66/1 Carnival Lad

racecards.race_name: BetWright Bangers N'Cash Maiden Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)

runners.breeder: Exors Of The Late Mr G J Freyne
runners.comment: Form of small-field bumper win is tricky to evaluate; still considered, nevertheless
runners.spotlight: Easily beat short-odds favourite in three-runner bumper for Don Cantillon in April; it's tricky to gauge what that form is worth and he was safely held at Galway festival for new stable last month; nevertheless, he has possibilities in this run-of-the-mill maiden on his hurdling debut.

runners.comment: Four-time AW winner; struggled when upped to 1m4f for stable debut; hurdle debut today
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, always in low-grade handicaps at around 1m; struggled when upped to 1m4f for recent stable debut and looks too risky on this first start over hurdles.

runners.comment: Well beaten (albeit on slow ground) on first two hurdling starts; returns from a layoff
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on bumper debut last October but well beaten (albeit on slow ground) on both subsequent hurdling starts; returns from 228-day absence here; not the most obvious answer.

runners.comment: A well-beaten outsider on all three hurdling starts for new stable this season
runners.spotlight: A well-beaten outsider on all three hurdling starts for new stable this season (twice over C&D); cannot be recommended.

runners.comment: Fifth foal from a low-grade hurdle winner; best watched on this belated debut
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal from a low-grade 2m/2m2f hurdle winner; best watched on this belated debut.

runners.comment: Disappointed on handicap debut but has good claims here if back on song for new stable
runners.spotlight: Sold for £10,000 after disappointing handicap debut for Jonjo and A J O'Neill in May but his clear second at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) a fortnight earlier was a pretty good effort, and he'll be heavily involved here if back on song for new stable.

runners.comment: Ran well for a long way when placed on hurdle debut at Fakenham last autumn; can feature
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to stable's top-class hurdler The New One; awarded small-field Newton Abbot bumper a year ago and ran well for a long way when third on subsequent hurdling debut at Fakenham (2m, good); can feature here if ready to roll after 11-month break.

runners.comment: Fair Flat racer; not beaten far after making steady late headway on both hurdling starts
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer; raced too freely at back of the field on both hurdling starts this summer but made steadily late headway and wasn't beaten far on either occasion; can improve as he gains experience over jumps, and the first-time hood may help him to relax here.

quotes.race: Best Racing Odds Guaranteed At BetMGM Handicap
quotes.quote: Caelan is still a big, raw horse. He won first-time out but ended up being quite immature and he was running a bit free but today he's done everything the right way round, although he wasn't doing a tap in front. He's taken well to all-weather surfaces but I don't see why he won't be just as effective on the grass - Hollie Doyle, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in three Irish points in early part of this year; rules debut
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise in three Irish points in early part of this year; could be considered only if attracting some market support before this rules debut.

runners.comment: Won course bumper by over 4l in June; major player on this hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when keeping on strongly for clearcut success in good-ground course bumper in July; major player if all goes to plan on this hurdling debut.

runners.comment: Tailed off in a bumper in December and also struggled in recent C&D hurdle for new stable
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Carlisle bumper for Mel Rowley in December and also struggled when switched to hurdling for new stable over C&D a fortnight ago; huge improvement needed.

runners.comment: Modest third in first two two Irish points in the spring; bought for £14,000 since
runners.spotlight: Kept on for modest third on Irish point debut in April but was pulled up on next outing; subsequently changed hands for £14,000; makes rules debut today.

runners.comment: Finished last on bumper debut in January and subsequently left Nigel Twiston-Davies
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful hurdler Milliner but finished last in Newcastle bumper (AW) in January and subsequently left Nigel Twiston-Davies.

runners.comment: Didn't show enough in two spring bumpers to warrant serious consideration
runners.spotlight: Didn't show enough in two spring bumpers to warrant serious consideration on this hurdling debut; cheekpieces (worn for both runs) now discarded.

runners.comment: Tailed off when 200-1 for hurdling debut at Sedgefield (2m4f) 11 days ago
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 200-1 for hurdling debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) 11 days ago; drops back in trip here.

racecards.verdict: Hurdling debutant UNCLE REMUS won a course bumper in pretty good style in July and gets the nod.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Uncle Remus, 3/1 The Expensive One, 4/1 Caelan, 5/1 American Empire, 16/1 Little Wren, 25/1 Sunshine Sally, 33/1 Flying Panther,  Harry Junior,  Lewisham Grove,  Truly Yours, 50/1 My Boy Beasty, 100/1 Lucky In Taipan,  Mc'flo,  Spent A Fortune

racecards.race_name: BetWright Bangers N'Cash Maiden Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)

runners.comment: Made quite promising bumper debut last October; now hurdling for new stable
runners.spotlight: Quite promising debut when fifth of 12 in soft-ground course bumper for David Simcock last October, after which he was sold for 19,500gns; makes stable/hurdle debut after 11-month absence.

runners.comment: Five-time Flat winner; not at best this year but may still be competitive on hurdle debut
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner at up to 2m; well below best on all three starts for new stable this year but can probably still be competitive at this sort of level if he takes well to hurdling.

quotes.race: Net World Sports 15th Anniversary Handicap
quotes.quote: Animato has his quirks but did it well the last day. I'd have liked to be handier today but he's got the talent and they went an honest gallop - Jason Watson, jockey, talking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Just a respectable third of five on hurdle debut; can improve but needs to
runners.spotlight: Placed on Irish point debut in 2024 but made underwhelming rules debut in a bumper in May and was just a respectable third of five over hurdles at Stratford (2m, good to firm) in July; can improve but needs to.

runners.comment: Good second over C&D on stable/hurdle debut; leading contender
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a bumper for James Moffatt in April but fared much better when second to odds-on favourite over C&D (good) on stable/hurdle debut at fortnight ago; that form already gives him strong claims today, and he has obvious scope to improve again.

runners.comment: Quite pleasing stable/hurdle debut when keeping-on third at Newton Abbot last month
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two of his three bumpers for Craig Lidster last season and made quite pleasing stable/hurdle debut when keeping-on third at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) last month; one to consider.

runners.comment: Placed on hurdle debut in Ireland but tailed off for new stable in November; now hooded
runners.spotlight: Placed in better maiden hurdle than this on debut for Colm Murphy at Wexford (2m4f, good) last July and was tailed off for new stable in November and has not raced again since; sports first-time hood; tricky to weigh up.

runners.comment: Stoutly bred 5yo who showed nothing more than minor promise in bumpers
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three bumpers, and his pedigree suggests we won't see the best of him until he tackles longer trips.

runners.comment: Posed no threat on his first two hurdling starts (2m/2m3f); look elsewhere
runners.spotlight: Posed no threat on his first two hurdling starts (2m/2m3f); look elsewhere.

runners.comment: Improved form in Irish bumpers this year, and also ran well in a 3m hurdle; stable debut
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in Irish bumpers but has shown significant improvement since the spring, making the frame three times; very respectable fifth of 18 when back over hurdles at Cork (3m, good) in July and has a fighting chance if he copes with the drop back to 2m on this stable debut.

runners.comment: Has shown some aptitude for hurdling but others in this field look stronger
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer; has shown some aptitude for hurdling, perhaps most notably when fourth of nine over C&D in June, but recent Flat form has been very disappointing and others in this field look stronger.

runners.comment: Second of five on Irish point debut in April, after which he changed hands for £16,000
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to useful hurdler Butler's Brief; second of five on Irish point debut in April (yielding ground), after which he changed hands for £16,000; market helpful on rules debut.

prev_owners.owner: Derrick Smith & Michael Tabor & Mrs John Magnier
Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Derrick Smith & Michael Tabor & Mrs John Magnier

runners.comment: Has the form to feature here but is 0-7 over hurdles and needs to better last month's run
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat in France last year but 0-7 and generally disappointing over hurdles for Paul Nicholls; has the form to feature here but was behind Mr McLoughlan when welll-held fourth at Newton Abbot last month and needs to up his game.

stable_tour.quote: Another of the French imports, who was bought at the Arqana Sale in July. He won his only Flat start at two for Andre Fabre, but rather lost his way this year. We've gelded him and he's recently back from a wind op, so won't be out in juvenile hurdles until after Christmas. 04-11-24

runners.comment: Well held before final-flight unseat here on last month's hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well before weakening into sixth of ten in good-ground course bumper in July, her second start, but was well held before final-flight unseat here on last month's hurdling debut.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both bumpers and makes no significant appeal on this hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in both bumpers and makes no significant appeal on this hurdling debut.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs G Rowland Clark & J O'Connor

runners.comment: Dual AW winner in March but always behind on recent hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Won two low-grade 1m4f AW handicaps for Michael Bell in March and ran okay on last month's stable debut but was always behind when switched to hurdling at Stratford nine days ago.

racecards.verdict: Second over C&D on his hurdling debut a fortnight ago, MOON OVER CLYRO will be hard to beat if he makes any further progress today.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Moon Over Clyro, 7/2 Mr McLoughlan, 11/2 Diamond Ambition,  Times A Wastin, 13/2 Roadshow, 12/1 Nels Son, 16/1 Aim Straight, 20/1 Belcamo,  Greer Hill, 25/1 Animato, 50/1 Forest Spirit, 66/1 Brooklyn Lullaby, 100/1 Osterberg,  Secret Vino,  Dyadoras Destiny

racecards.race_name: BetWright All Bun & Games Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Ran big race when clear second over C&D a fortnight ago; high on the list
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner who has worked his way back into form over hurdles this season, following a long layoff; ran big race when clear second over C&D (good) a fortnight ago and commands respect.

runners.comment: Much better known as a chaser but could have a say off lower hurdle mark for new stable
runners.spotlight: Won over fences for Barry Brennan last summer and has posted a couple of good efforts in that sphere this season; reverts to hurdling off lower mark for new stable here; could have a say.

quotes.race: Happy Birthday Jo Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Great d'Ange has been swimming and I think the Jersey race brought him on a bit. He jumps and he needs a fence, so that's exactly what he got today. Today proves that he's actually happy and he's enjoying it. The more Martin let him bowl along, the more he jumped and the more confidence he got. There has been a lot of work put into this horse, so it's great that it happened - Barry Brennan, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Fitzdares Wishes Everyone A Merry Christmas Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: It took a while for everything to come together for Great D'Ange. He was always a good jumper but was just getting a few uncharacteristic mistakes. The more jumps the better now because he really enjoys it and hopefully he'll keep progressing. He likes good ground on a flat track and a test of jumping - Tom George, trainer.

runners.comment: Back from mammoth absence this summer; latter of two runs proved ability remains
runners.spotlight: Back from mammoth absence with two runs this summer; looked held when falling on first occasion but last month's fifth at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) was much more encouraging, and he might take another step forward if his stamina holds out here.

quotes.race: racingtv.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Dreamweaver has improved for being gelded over the winter and going up in trip. He's a late-maturing type, who is very honest and straightforward - Liam Keniry, rider.

quotes.race: Long View Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: I think Dreamweaver has a bright future. I'm not saying he'll be a stakes horse but his dam Livia's Dream started off in handicaps off the same sort of mark and went on to win a Listed race at five. We gelded him because he was just a bit silly, and he was never going to be a stallion and so he didn't need them - Ed Walker, trainer

stable_tour.quote: He's a solid handicapper who needs really quick ground and never really got it last season. I trained his dam and she got better and better with time and I hope he'll carry on improving, he looks that sort. 22-04-21

runners.comment: Dual chase winner in February; has possibilities on this first hurdling start since 2021
runners.spotlight: Struck form with two 3m chase wins a Ludlow (good to soft/soft) in February, and returned from six-month break with satisfactory run here (2m7f, good) a fortnight ago; unraced over hurdles since 2021 but has possibilities if that recent outing brought him on, and rain will aid his cause.

stable_tour.quote: He came to us after racing in France as well as a spell with Ed de Giles in England and had shown glimpses of form but not got his head in front. We gave him one run last season before putting him away and bringing him back at Stratford, where he ran a nice race to be third over 2m3½f. Stepping him up to 3m clearly had the desired effect as he won next time out at Ffos Las. It was a big step forward from him and clearly staying chasing is his game. He jumped very well at the Welsh track and got every inch of the 3m. We could head up to Newcastle on Thursday with him as there a couple of options there for him. He is up to a mark of 100 now and I would like to think from what he has done so far that there is more in the tank. 21-11-22

runners.comment: Safely held on recent stable/chase debut; reverts to hurdling with a bit to prove
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles when based in Ireland and safely held when fifth on recent stable/chase debut; may yet come good for Mickey Bowen but reverts to hurdling with a fair bit to prove.

prev_owners.owner: G Strawbridge, J Wigan & T Hirosaki

runners.comment: Scored over 2m2f in June and this return to a staying trip is arguably overdue
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) in June, his second start for Sophie Leech; just a respectable fourth of 12 at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) last month but this return to a staying trip is arguably overdue (stays at least 3m2f); Sean Bowen takes the ride; one to consider.

runners.comment: Yet to build upon ready win in May but ran well for a long way when third last month
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement when winning readily on stable debut at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in May; yet to build on that performance but there have been some excuses and she travelled well for a long way when third at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last month; no surprise if she features.

runners.comment: Opened account at Hexham in June and both subsequent defeats can be excused
runners.spotlight: Returned to racing under rules with two good runs in spring, notably when opening account at Hexham (2m7f, good) on latter occasion; reportedly in season when below form on next outing and did not have race run to suit when dropped back to 2m4f last time; remains of interest.

quotes.race: Greenhead Hotel Novices' Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Belladinotte is quite a quirky mare and we've just made her happy. She gets treated like the princess she is and we've just given her TLC. She's blossomed. From our last five jumpers we've had a winner, three seconds and then another winner. They're absolutely flying - Sara Ender, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Clear second over 2m4f here on recent handicap debut; up in trip today; major player
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in her three qualifying races in the spring but travelled well for a long way when clear second on recent handicap debut here (2m4f, good); upped in trip today and could easily have more to offer.

runners.comment: First two stable runs were encouraging; pulled up since and now 0-20 over hurdles
runners.spotlight: Started off for new stable with two encouraging runs in spring but was pulled up on latest outing 11 weeks ago and is now 0-20 over hurdles; needs recent wind op to have made a difference.

runners.comment: No promise in 2m maidens but pedigree suggests this trip may unlock improvement
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise in three 2m maiden hurdles at this track but has lowly mark for handicap debut and pedigree suggests the step up in trip will unlock improvement; swaps cheekpieces for blinkers.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is SEA THRIFT, who moves up in trip after running very well in defeat over 2m4f here on her recent handicap debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Sea Thrift, 7/2 Chief Black Robe, 5/1 Stiletto, 7/1 Lyness Dancer, 10/1 Faitque de L'Isle,  Belladinotte, 12/1 Great d'Ange,  Dreamweaver, 14/1 Getaway With You, 25/1 Oi Oi Oi

racecards.race_name: BetWright Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.breeder: Societe Civile De L'Ecurie De Meautry
runners.comment: Started well for this yard but struggled the last twice after surging up the weights
runners.spotlight: Typically for new recruits for this yard, he hit the ground running but has struggled the last twice after surging up the weights; others appeal more.

stable_tour.quote: We bought him from France and he was very disappointing at the start of the year. The mistake we probably made was trying him as a juvenile hurdler as it didn't work. He was fit and we kept going with him, but sometimes horses from France need time to acclimatise and come to themselves. He's got a lot to prove and has shown nothing since we've had him, but he's a rarity in that his hurdles mark is lower than his Flat one. We're going to try to run him in a handicap hurdle in November after his summer break. If it clicks with him, he could be thrown in. 26-09-24

runners.breeder: A O'Brien,M Al Monsour& K Al' Attiyah
runners.comment: 1-12 over hurdles; kicks off for the Skeltons on a competitive mark; interesting
runners.spotlight: 1-12 over hurdles; kicks off for the Skeltons on a competitive mark and has won in the reapplied tongue-tie; the recent rain won't hinder his chances and of obvious interest.

quotes.race: Follow Us On Social Media Maiden
quotes.quote: Ghasham is a massive big horse and looks progressive. It was a nice performance and there is a lot of improvement to come from him. He loved that ground and was very tough - Brendan Powell, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Good days are hard to predict but rallied to push an idling Orange Diamond close here
runners.spotlight: 3-39 over hurdles; good days are hard to predict but rallied to push an idling Orange Diamond close here last month; risky but capable.

quotes.race: COLM QUINN BMW Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
quotes.quote: Royal Rhythm got a fantastic ride from James because in my mind the others went way too quick and they were racing too early. He kept taking him back and he probably won too well, he was the only one that finished - Philip Rothwell, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.comment: Placed form over hurdles and recently second on the Flat after wind surgery
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but has placed form and was runner-up after a break back (and wind surgery) on the Flat at Brighton two weeks ago (1m4f, good); could have some say.

runners.comment: Idled here last time; has the ability to deal with the 6lb rise if maintaining his focus
runners.spotlight: Dual winner in France; 2-2 for this yard after narrow wins over C&D (good) and some late idling didn't help the cause last time when repelling Royal Rhythm; probably has the ability to deal with the 6lb rise if he can maintain his focus when it matters.

runners.comment: Quiet so far over hurdles but handicap debut and she's a three-time Flat winner
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner at up to 1m6f; quiet so far over hurdles but she could be a different proposition now sent into handicaps; market may be informative.

runners.comment: Only 2lb higher than for May's win at Uttoxeter and fitness shouldn't be an issue
runners.spotlight: Winning for a fourth time at Uttoxeter in May, by a half length over 2m4f on good ground; only 2lb higher and fitness shouldn't an issue.

runners.comment: Has shown promise in a handicap but offered little on her return from a long absence
runners.spotlight: Running well in her second handicap when knocked out of contention in May 2024 but then went missing for 400 days and was tailed off on her return three months ago; could come good at some point but likely best watched here.

runners.breeder: E A R L Haras De Montaigu
runners.comment: 0-10; second run for the yard when didn't run badly for a 66-1 chance at Market Rasen
runners.spotlight: Left Ben Pauling for £6,500 and as an eight-race maiden; didn't run badly for a 66-1 chance at Market Rasen last time (2m2f, good) and that effort offers some hope.

runners.comment: C&D winner in 2023 but needed to show more back here in July after a long absence
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023 but needed to show more back here in July after a long absence.

racecards.verdict: It will be interesting to see what Dan Skelton can do with GHASHAM who is on a competitive mark on his best form.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Ghasham, 7/2 Orange Diamond, 4/1 Rock On Tommy, 8/1 Ask Peter, 10/1 Mr Le Philosophe,  Royal Rhythm,  Blue Universe, 14/1 Jamaicaine, 16/1 Platenium, 25/1 Sir Jack West

racecards.race_name: BetWright Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.comment: Pulled up over C&D on recent stable debut and needs that run to have brought him on
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on pick of form for Jonjo and A J O'Neill but became inconsistent for that stable and was pulled up over C&D (good) for new yard last month; needs to have taken significant benefit from that recent run.

stable_tour.quote: A lovely individual who wants soft ground. He'll start over hurdles over two and a half miles at a nice galloping track like Ffos Las, Chepstow or Exeter. He's a nice horse who wants fences really next year. 25-10-22

runners.comment: Absent since clear second on handicap debut in November; big player if revved up
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who was a long way clear of the others when second of five behind dual subsequent winner on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) last November; absent since and likely to encounter faster ground here but has good claims if fully tuned up.

runners.comment: Absent since poor run in April but was creditable third on handicap debut two starts ago
runners.spotlight: Absent since running as though something was amiss in April but was creditable third on handicap debut at Hereford (2m3f, good) in March and may still have potential.

runners.comment: Very respectable fifth of 15 on handicap debut in Ireland (2m); now with new stable
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles when based in Ireland but kept on for very respectable fifth of 15 on handicap debut at Roscommon (2m, good) in July; back up in trip for stable debut (same owner); still open to improvement.

runners.comment: Back from break with two fairly good runs this summer; unexposed over today's trip
runners.spotlight: Ended 2024 with narrow win at Hereford (2m, good to soft) and returned from break with two fairly good runs at Stratford (2m2f/2m) this summer; needs to find extra here but is unexposed over the trip.

runners.comment: Placed twice at Newton Abbot since win there in June; may find others better handicapped
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good to soft) in June, his fourth hurdling success, and has been placed there twice since (2m2f, good); might find others better handicapped here but should be fine over the trip.

runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles in Ireland and probably best watched on this stable debut
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight hurdling starts in Ireland and absent since beaten out of sight on heavy ground in February; had wind surgery during the summer but is probably best watched on this stable debut.

runners.comment: Shaped well over C&D on stable debut in July but others in this line-up appeal more
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; shaped quite well when fourth over C&D on stable debut in July, after a layoff, and subsequent Newton Abbot run wasn't anything like as bad as eventual 44l defeat might suggest (eased after bad jumping error); others in this line-up appeal more, though.

prev_owners.owner: J Bedi, P Ellerby And Partners

runners.comment: Two convincing hurdle wins for new yard; strong claims if reverting to fences here
runners.spotlight: Evidently revitalised by switch to current stable when sauntering clear to win by 20l over hurdles at Fontwell last week (2m5f, good; has also won on soft); cosily landed the odds at Worcester yesterday and would have strong claims if he reverts to chasing here.

quotes.race: Kilmanahan Beginners Chase
quotes.quote: Shantou Lucky ran very well at Leopardstown last time and he is taking to fences. He will stay on the go probably but he might not run on good ground - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well enough at Fairyhouse this month. He'll mix and match between hurdles and fences and hopefully pay his way. 27-10-22

prev_owners.owner: Whose Round Is It Now Syndicate

runners.comment: Struggled on first two starts for new stable this summer and is now 0-15 overall
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first two starts for new stable this summer (2m2f/2m3f) and is now 0-15 overall.

racecards.verdict: James Owen's SHANTOU LUCKY easily won a conditional jockeys' race by 20l on his recent stable debut and runs without a penalty here.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/5 Shantou Lucky, 7/1 Diamond Geezer, 8/1 Sassified, 14/1 Reinado, 16/1 Tracking Treasure,  Lady Henrietta, 20/1 Timeforarum, 33/1 Copper Cove, 50/1 Another Question, 100/1 Across The Ocean
